# Dossier 4: Hillary Diane Rodham Clinton

Hillary Diane Rodham Clinton was born on 26 October 1947 in Chicago, Illinois, the daughter of Hugh Ellsworth Rodham, a textile-business owner, and Dorothy Howell Rodham. She graduated from Maine South High School in 1965, took her BA in political science from Wellesley College in 1969 (where she was student-government president and commencement speaker), and her JD from Yale Law School in 1973. In 1974 she served as a staff attorney on the House Judiciary Committee's impeachment inquiry into Richard Nixon. She married Bill Clinton on 11 October 1975 in Fayetteville, Arkansas. She became a partner at the Rose Law Firm in Little Rock in 1979. She served as First Lady of Arkansas 1979-1981 and 1983-1992 across Bill Clinton's gubernatorial tenures. She served as First Lady of the United States from 20 January 1993 to 20 January 2001. She served as the junior United States Senator from New York from 3 January 2001 to 21 January 2009. She served as the 67th United States Secretary of State from 21 January 2009 to 1 February 2013. She was the Democratic Party nominee for President in 2016 and lost the general election to Donald Trump on 8 November 2016.

The Hillary Clinton biographical record is the public-facing record of the late-twentieth-century Democratic-Party institutional female political-class operator. The Wellesley-and-Yale-Law trajectory; the 1974 Watergate staff position; the Arkansas First Lady period across the 1980s; the 1993-2001 White House First Lady period that the captured commercial press treated as the precursor to the 2008 presidential candidacy; the 2001-2009 Senate tenure; the 2008 Democratic primary loss to Obama; the 2009-2013 Secretary of State tenure; the 2016 Democratic presidential nomination; the 8 November 2016 general-election loss to Donald Trump that produced the Trump first presidency. The governing record is the documented record of what the political-class operator did with the access of the Democratic-party-and-American-state offices across the fifty-year career. The biographical record is the public-facing presentation. The record shows the real operation of that power.

## The Rose Law Firm and the Whitewater period (1979-1992)

The Arkansas commercial-political record of the pre-Presidency Hillary Clinton was the Rose Law Firm partnership and the Whitewater Development Corporation investment. The Clintons invested in the Whitewater real-estate venture in 1978 with James and Susan McDougal. The venture was associated with the Madison Guaranty Savings & Loan, the savings-and-loan institution operated by James McDougal that subsequently failed in the savings-and-loan crisis of the late 1980s and cost American taxpayers roughly $60 million in deposit-insurance liability. The Rose Law Firm represented Madison Guaranty across the period of Hillary Clinton's partnership at the firm. The billing-records controversy across the 1993-1998 Whitewater investigation period produced the subpoena of the Rose Law Firm billing records that the Clinton White House initially could not locate; the billing records subsequently appeared in the White House residence book room in January 1996, two years after they had been subpoenaed.

The Independent Counsel investigation under Robert Fiske (1994) and Kenneth Starr (1994-2002) produced no indictment of Bill or Hillary Clinton. The investigation produced fifteen convictions including those of James and Susan McDougal on Whitewater fraud charges and Webster Hubbell, Hillary Clinton's former Rose Law Firm partner who served as Associate Attorney General under Bill Clinton, on tax and embezzlement charges. The pattern of the Whitewater period — the commercial-political arrangement involving the Arkansas savings-and-loan and the Arkansas governing class, the federal investigation, the fifteen convictions of figures around the Clintons that did not extend to the Clintons themselves — was the recurring pattern of the Clinton political-class career that the dossier sections document at the federal level.

## First Ladyship 1993-2001

The 1993-2001 White House period was the first national-political operation of Hillary Clinton in her independent political-class capacity. The signature programmatic initiative of the period was the Task Force on National Health Care Reform.

### The Task Force on National Health Care Reform

The Task Force was established by Bill Clinton on 25 January 1993, five days into his presidency, with Hillary Clinton as Chair. The Task Force operated across the nine months of its closed-session deliberations, producing the Clinton health-reform legislation that was introduced into the 103rd Congress in November 1993. The legislation was defeated in September 1994 without a floor vote in either chamber. The defeat was attributed in the political-class analysis of the time to the insurance-industry "Harry and Louise" advertising campaign, the procedural mishandling by the Task Force (which operated in closed sessions across 1993 and produced the "Hillarycare" public-relations characterisation that the commercial press picked up), and the centrist-Democratic-and-Republican congressional opposition. The consequence of the health-reform defeat was the Democratic-Party 1994 midterm-election loss of both chambers of Congress to the Republican Party — the first Republican congressional majority since 1954. The 1994 Republican wave was directly attributed in the post-election analysis of the time to the failure of the health-care reform programme.

The structural point of the Hillary Clinton Task Force period is that the political-class operator who would, twenty years later, take $225,000 per speech from the American private health-insurance industry across the 2013-2015 paid-speech period had, in her first independent political-class operation in 1993-1994, attempted to construct a healthcare-reform programme that would have rearranged the American private health-insurance industry's post-1945 commercial position. The 1993-1994 attempt failed. The 2013-2015 paid speeches succeeded. The 2016 presidential campaign's public-facing position against the single-payer healthcare alternative was the position of the 2013-2015 paid-speech period, not the 1993-1994 Task Force period. The position evolution across the twenty-year period is documented.

### Travelgate

The May 1993 White House Travel Office firings produced the first major Hillary Clinton ethical controversy of the First Ladyship. Seven White House Travel Office employees were dismissed; the firings produced allegations that Hillary Clinton had pressed for them in order to redirect the travel-services business to a Clinton-friendly travel agency (World Wide Travel of Little Rock, Arkansas). The 1996 House Government Reform and Oversight Committee Republican investigation concluded that Hillary Clinton had played a role in the firings, contradicting the contemporaneous Clinton public statements that she had not been involved. The 2000 Independent Counsel Robert Ray report concluded that Hillary Clinton had given "factually false" statements about her involvement, but the false statements did not rise to the criminal-prosecution threshold. The Travelgate pattern — public denial of involvement followed by subsequent disclosure that the involvement had been the factual reality — was the recurring pattern of the political-class career.

### Vince Foster

On 20 July 1993, Vince Foster, the Deputy White House Counsel and former Rose Law Firm partner of Hillary Clinton, was found dead at Fort Marcy Park, Virginia, of a gunshot wound to the head. The official conclusion across five separate investigations — the US Park Police, the Department of Justice, the Special Counsel Robert Fiske, the Independent Counsel Kenneth Starr, and the Senate Whitewater Committee — was that the death was suicide. The conspiracy theories surrounding the Foster death have continued to circulate throughout the post-1993 American political-class commentary; the official investigations have remained the official record. The point of this dossier is that the official record is the evidentiary baseline; the conspiracy-theory commentary is the post-official-investigation analytical-class production that is not the subject of this dossier.

## Senate 2001-2009

The 2001-2009 Senate tenure of Hillary Clinton operated through the conventional Democratic-Party institutional positioning of the incoming junior Senator from a major American state who was the obvious 2008 presidential candidate from the moment of her 2000 senatorial election. The signature legislative votes of the eight-year tenure were the 2001 USA PATRIOT Act vote (yes), the 2002 Iraq AUMF vote (yes), and the 2007-2008 financial-crisis legislative votes including the October 2008 TARP vote (yes).

The 11 October 2002 Iraq AUMF vote was the defining vote of the Hillary Clinton Senate tenure. Senator Clinton voted for the Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution, the congressional authorisation for the 2003 invasion of Iraq. The Clinton vote became the political-class issue of the 2007-2008 Democratic primary campaign, in which the Obama campaign contrasted Obama's then-state-senator 2002 anti-war public statements with Clinton's 2002 yes vote. The contrast was the decisive issue of the Iowa caucus victory by Obama in January 2008 and the subsequent Obama primary momentum that ended the Clinton 2008 presidential candidacy.

Clinton publicly described her 2002 vote as a "mistake" in her 2014 memoir *Hard Choices*. The apology came twelve years after the vote, six years after the Iraq vote had cost her the 2008 Democratic nomination. The pattern — late acknowledgment of the contemporaneous political-class position that the post-event political-class analysis had identified as the wrong position — was the recurring pattern of the career.

The 2001-2009 Senate banking record included Clinton's 2001-2002 service on the Senate Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs Committee. The 2007-2008 election cycle's Wall Street fundraising for Clinton totalled roughly $733,000 from the securities-and-investment sector — among the largest 2008 primary cycle Wall Street fundraising totals. The arrangement was the pre-2008 Hillary Clinton senatorial financial-services-industry support that the 2013-2015 paid-speech period would build on.

## Secretary of State 2009-2013

The Hillary Clinton Secretary of State tenure (21 January 2009 - 1 February 2013) was the diplomatic-and-commercial operation that established the Clinton-family commercial portfolio across the 2009-2016 period.

### The pre-Confirmation ethics arrangement

In December 2008, before her confirmation as Secretary of State, Hillary Clinton signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Obama administration regarding the Clinton Foundation's foreign-donor relationships. The MOU prohibited new foreign-government donations to the Foundation during Clinton's Secretary of State tenure unless the State Department ethics officials approved them; the MOU permitted continuing donations from foreign governments that had donated to the Foundation prior to the MOU. The Republican on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee at the January 2009 confirmation hearing, Senator Richard Lugar of Indiana, said the Clinton Foundation was "a unique complication that would have to be managed with great care and transparency"; the Foundation could create "the impression that foreign donors were giving money to gain access at the State Department."

### Foreign-government donations during the Secretary tenure

The Clinton Foundation accepted donations from at least seven foreign governments during Clinton's Secretary tenure (2009-2013) in continuation of pre-existing donor relationships permitted under the MOU: Australia, the Dominican Republic, Kuwait, Norway, Qatar, Oman, and the United Kingdom. The cumulative donation totals from the foreign-government donors across the 2009-2013 period were in the multimillion-dollar range. The Algerian donation — a $500,000 donation to the Foundation's Haiti relief fund in 2010 — was accepted without the State Department ethics review the MOU required. The *Washington Post* 25 February 2015 reporting documented that the Algerian donation "coincided with a spike" in Algerian government lobbying of the State Department on the Algerian human-rights record. The Clinton Health Access Initiative (CHAI), the subsidiary of the Clinton Foundation, failed to report donors from 2009 to 2013 — a violation of the MOU disclosure requirements that the CHAI spokesperson attributed to "oversight."

### The UBS settlement

The 2009 IRS investigation of UBS, the Swiss bank, over the offshore-tax-shelter accounts of roughly 52,000 Americans was the tax-enforcement priority of the Obama Justice Department and Treasury at the moment of Clinton's Secretary appointment. The February 2009 settlement, brokered by Clinton in her Secretary of State capacity, produced UBS's agreement to provide the identities of 4,450 American account holders out of the 52,000 the IRS had requested — under 10% of the IRS request total.

The post-settlement commercial-political sequence was documented of the April 2015 *Wall Street Journal* reporting by Grimaldi and Ballhaus: UBS's cumulative donations to the Clinton Foundation grew from less than $60,000 through 2008 to roughly $600,000 by end of 2014. UBS additionally paid Bill Clinton $1.5 million for a series of question-and-answer sessions with UBS Wealth Management CEO Bob McCann across 2011-2014 — the largest single corporate-speech-income source for Bill Clinton in the post-Presidency period. The sequence — IRS settlement brokered by the Secretary of State, ten-fold increase in UBS Clinton Foundation donations, $1.5 million in Bill Clinton speaking fees from the settled bank — was the contemporaneous documented commercial-political arrangement between the Secretary's family foundation and the multinational bank whose IRS investigation the Secretary had brokered the settlement of. The arrangement is documented.

### Libya and Benghazi

The March 2011 NATO intervention in Libya is covered in Dossier 3 (Obama). Clinton was the diplomatic principal of the intervention. Her post-Gaddafi 20 October 2011 CBS News public statement — "We came, we saw, he died" — is documented. Obama's April 2016 acknowledgment that the failure to plan for post-Gaddafi Libya was "the worst mistake" of his presidency is documented. The Libyan trajectory since the post-2011 period — the multi-faction civil war, the collapse of the North African oil producer, the post-2014 Mediterranean migration crisis that drove the post-2015 European political-class realignment — was the documented consequence of the Clinton-era diplomatic operation.

The 11 September 2012 Benghazi attack — the coordinated armed attack on the US diplomatic compound and the CIA annex at Benghazi that killed US Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens, Foreign Service Information Management Officer Sean Smith, and CIA contractors Tyrone S. Woods and Glen Doherty — produced the 2012-2016 post-Benghazi investigation sequence: the Accountability Review Board (the State Department internal review, December 2012); the six congressional investigations including the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence and the House Select Committee on Benghazi (2014-2016 under Chairman Trey Gowdy). The cumulative investigations produced criticism of the State Department's pre-attack security posture but did not produce findings of criminal misconduct by Hillary Clinton. The 22 October 2015 Hillary Clinton testimony — the eleven-hour public testimony before the House Select Committee on Benghazi — was the public-record moment at which the Clinton 2016 presidential campaign survived the Benghazi political-class controversy.

### The private email-server arrangement

In January 2009, Hillary Clinton established a private email server (clintonemail.com) operated from her Chappaqua, New York residence for her State Department official correspondence. The arrangement bypassed the State.gov system that the State Department's records-retention policy required for official correspondence. The public disclosure of the arrangement came in March 2015 through the *New York Times* reporting. The FBI investigation under Director James Comey across 2015-2016 examined roughly 30,000 emails from the server. The findings: at least 110 emails contained classified information at the time they were sent; eight email chains contained Top Secret information. The 5 July 2016 FBI Director Comey statement was that Clinton and her aides had been "extremely careless" in their handling of classified information, but the conduct did not rise to the criminal-prosecution threshold; the FBI recommended against indictment.

The 28 October 2016 Comey letter — eleven days before the 8 November 2016 election — publicly announced that the FBI was reopening the email investigation based on new emails discovered on Anthony Weiner's laptop (Weiner's wife, Huma Abedin, was Clinton's aide). The 6 November 2016 Comey follow-up letter, two days before the election, announced that the new emails had been reviewed and did not change the July recommendation. The 28 October-to-6 November sequence was the late-2016 election-cycle event that Clinton in her 2017 memoir *What Happened* identified as the decisive event of the 2016 election.

### Uranium One

Across 2009-2013, Uranium One — a Canadian uranium-mining company with US mining stakes including roughly 20% of US uranium-production capacity — was acquired in stages by Rosatom, the Russian state atomic-energy corporation. The acquisition required approval from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS), which included the State Department as one of the nine agencies on the committee.

During the 2008-2013 period of the Uranium One acquisition negotiations, individuals associated with the Uranium One transaction — including Uranium One Chairman Ian Telfer and figures from the Frank Giustra Mining Group — donated amounts totalling roughly $145 million to the Clinton Foundation. The donations were not publicly disclosed at the time of the CFIUS approval despite the Clinton Foundation public-disclosure obligations. The 23 April 2015 *New York Times* investigation by Jo Becker and Mike McIntire, "Cash Flowed to Clinton Foundation Amid Russian Uranium Deal," established the record of the parallel commercial-and-political arrangements.

The 2017-2018 Trump Department of Justice review of the Uranium One transaction did not produce criminal charges. The Republican-investigative position was that the absence of charges reflected post-2017 institutional protection rather than the absence of factual misconduct. The Democratic-investigative position was that the CFIUS approval had been a nine-agency decision in which the State Department was one vote among nine; the individual Clinton role in the decision was, on the evidence, the subject of contested interpretation. The conclusion is documentary: the parallel arrangement existed, the $145 million in Clinton Foundation donations from the parties to the transaction occurred, the CFIUS approval was given, and the factual record of the parallel arrangement is on the public record regardless of the legal-and-investigative-class interpretation.

## The 2016 presidential campaign

### The Democratic primary and the DNC tilt

The 12 February - 14 June 2016 Democratic primary contest was between Hillary Clinton and Senator Bernie Sanders of Vermont. Clinton won 16.9 million primary votes to Sanders's 13.2 million; Clinton won the pledged-delegate count 2,205 to 1,846; Clinton's superdelegate lead at the commencement of the primary cycle was roughly 458 to 7. The Democratic primary was the contest in which the Clinton 2008 institutional Democratic-party support apparatus was the 2016 Clinton support apparatus from the beginning.

The 22 July 2016 WikiLeaks DNC email release — roughly 20,000 DNC emails dating from January 2015 through May 2016, published two days before the Democratic National Convention — included DNC officials discussing the Sanders primary campaign with contempt and proposing ways to undermine Sanders' candidacy (including the proposal to raise questions about Sanders' Jewish faith in Southern primary states). The content of the emails confirmed the Sanders-campaign-and-Sanders-supporter pre-leak allegations that the DNC had not maintained the procedural neutrality required of the DNC under the DNC charter. On 24 July 2016, DNC Chair Debbie Wasserman Schultz announced her resignation effective at the end of the Democratic Convention. The resignation was the public acknowledgment of the procedural impropriety the emails had documented. Donna Brazile, the DNC Vice-Chair, became the interim DNC Chair.

The post-2016 Donna Brazile disclosures established the structural arrangement. In her 2017 memoir *Hacks: The Inside Story of the Break-ins and Breakdowns That Put Donald Trump in the White House*, Brazile documented the August 2015 Joint Fundraising Agreement between the Clinton campaign, the DNC, and the Hillary Victory Fund — an agreement that, on Brazile's documentary record, gave the Clinton campaign control over the DNC operations and staff hiring across the 2015-2016 primary cycle. The Brazile conclusion on the Joint Fundraising Agreement: "the funding arrangement... Was not illegal, but it sure looked unethical." The arrangement was the structural mechanism through which the Clinton campaign operated the DNC as a Clinton-campaign extension across the 2015-2016 primary cycle.

### The Wall Street speaking-fee record

The 13-month post-State-Department speaking-fee period, January 2014 to March 2015, produced 53 paid speeches at a standard fee of $225,000, totalling roughly $11.8 million in fees. The 2013 speaking-fee period (pre-disclosure) included the three Goldman Sachs speeches at $225,000 each totalling $675,000, plus speeches to Morgan Stanley, Deutsche Bank, UBS, and other Wall Street firms. The cumulative 2013-2015 paid-speech period produced 92 paid speeches totalling roughly $21.6 million per the CNN Money analysis. Eight speeches to major banks netted $1.8 million per the same analysis.

The 7 October 2016 publication by *The Intercept* of excerpts from the Goldman Sachs and other speaking-fee transcripts — released through the WikiLeaks publication of Clinton campaign chair John Podesta's emails — documented Clinton's statements at the 2013 paid events. The most-quoted statement was the public-vs-private-position statement to a 2013 housing trade group: "If everybody's watching, you know, all of the back room discussions and the deals, you know, then people get a little nervous, to say the least. So, you need both a public and a private position." The statement was the documented Clinton acknowledgment of the dual-position political-class operational mode. The April 2013 Morgan Stanley speech included Clinton's endorsement of the Simpson-Bowles deficit reduction plan that included corporate-tax-rate reductions and Social Security retirement-age increases — an endorsement that contradicted her 2016 public campaign positions against the Simpson-Bowles proposals.

The Sanders-campaign pressure for transcript release across the 2016 primary period was the public-pressure mechanism through which the content of the speeches became the 2016 election-cycle public-record issue. Clinton publicly committed during the 2016 primary to release the Goldman Sachs transcripts only if all other 2016 candidates released their speech transcripts — the commitment that allowed Clinton to avoid disclosing the content of the Goldman Sachs speeches until the 7 October 2016 WikiLeaks-Podesta-email publication forced the disclosure.

### The 8 November 2016 election

The 8 November 2016 election produced Donald Trump's election with 304 Electoral College votes to Hillary Clinton's 227. Clinton won the national popular vote by roughly 2.9 million votes (65,853,514 to 62,984,828). Trump won the Electoral College through narrow victories in Pennsylvania (44,292 vote margin), Wisconsin (22,748 vote margin), and Michigan (10,704 vote margin) — a combined 77,744-vote margin in three Midwestern states that the Clinton campaign had not visited in the closing weeks of the campaign in the assumption that the Clinton "blue wall" would hold.

The post-election Clinton analysis, in her 2017 memoir *What Happened*, identified the 28 October Comey letter, the Russian interference (the 2017 US Intelligence Community Assessment of Russian interference in the 2016 election), the misogyny of the American electorate, and the structural deficiencies of the Democratic Party as the 2016 election factors. The post-2016 left-Democratic analysis (Thomas Frank, *Listen, Liberal*; Matt Stoller; the Sanders-aligned analytical class) reached the alternative conclusion: the Clinton campaign had failed to make the economic-populist case to the post-2008 Rust Belt working-class American voter; the campaign had been built on the identity-politics-and-celebrity-endorsement apparatus; the Wall Street speaking-fee record had been the structural credibility problem the campaign had been unable to overcome.

The documented conclusion is that both analyses are partially correct on the documentary record. The Comey letter timing was the late-cycle proximate event that moved the margin in the swing states. The Wall Street speaking-fee record and the Clinton Foundation foreign-donation record were the structural credibility problems that had been built into the 2009-2015 Hillary Clinton commercial-political portfolio across the pre-2016 period. The structural and the proximate factors operated together. The arrangement is documented.

## Post-2016

Clinton's 2017 memoir *What Happened* was published September 2017. The 2017-2020 period included the Onward Together political-action group, the post-2016 public commentary on the post-2016 American political-class period, and the 2020 Democratic Electoral College certification of Joe Biden's election. The 2023-present period includes the Hillary Rodham Clinton Presidential Fellows programme at Columbia University and the 2024-2025 public commentary on the post-2024 Trump second-presidency period.

## What it means

Hillary Diane Rodham Clinton was, across the fifty-year career from the 1974 Watergate staff position to the 2025-present post-Secretary commentary, the Democratic-Party institutional female political-class operator of the post-1968 American political-class settlement. The institutional record runs through four phases.

Arkansas and First Lady (1974-2001): the Rose Law Firm partnership and the Whitewater commercial arrangement that produced fifteen convictions of figures around the Clintons without charges against the Clintons themselves; the 1993-1994 Health Care Task Force that the insurance industry defeated; and the 1993 Travelgate and the 2000 Independent Counsel finding of "factually false" Clinton statements.

Senate and Iraq vote (2001-2008): the 2002 Iraq AUMF vote that Clinton apologised for twelve years later.

Secretary of State (2009-2013): the tenure that built the Clinton Foundation foreign-donation portfolio, brokered the UBS settlement that produced the $1.5 million Bill Clinton speaking-fees-from-UBS sequence, presided over the 2011 Libya intervention that produced the post-Gaddafi Libyan civil war and the post-2014 Mediterranean migration crisis, and operated the private-email-server arrangement that produced the 2016 election-cycle FBI investigation.

Speaking-fee period and 2016 loss (2013-2016): the 2013-2015 Wall Street speaking-fee period that produced the $21.6 million in fees and the "public and a private position" Clinton acknowledgment of the dual-position political-class mode; the 2016 Democratic primary in which the DNC operated as a Clinton-campaign extension; and the 8 November 2016 general-election loss to Donald Trump.

Fifty years of institutional access. The $21.6 million in Goldman Sachs speaking fees was the quiet part. The private email server was the careless part. The 2016 loss was the part the electorate decided.

# Dossier 5: William Jefferson Clinton

William Jefferson Clinton was born on 19 August 1946 in Hope, Arkansas, as William Jefferson Blythe III. His father, William Jefferson Blythe Jr., died in an automobile accident three months before Bill's birth. His mother, Virginia Cassidy Blythe, married Roger Clinton Sr. In 1950; Bill formally adopted the Clinton surname in 1962. He graduated from Hot Springs High School in 1964, took his BA from the Georgetown University School of Foreign Service in 1968, was a Rhodes Scholar at University College, Oxford 1968-1970, and took his JD from Yale Law School in 1973. He married Hillary Rodham on 11 October 1975 in Fayetteville, Arkansas. He served as Arkansas Attorney General 1977-1979, as Governor of Arkansas 1979-1981 and 1983-1992 (he lost re-election in 1980 to Republican Frank White and regained the office in 1982), and as the 42nd President of the United States from 20 January 1993 to 20 January 2001. He founded the William J. Clinton Foundation in 2001 (renamed the Bill, Hillary & Chelsea Clinton Foundation in 2013).

The Bill Clinton biographical record is the public-facing record of the post-1968 American Democratic-party operator who built the Third Way Democratic-party model that the Blair Labour Party in Britain, the Schröder SPD in Germany, and the subsequent Obama-Biden-Hillary Clinton sequence in the United States operated. The Rhodes-Scholar-Yale-Law-Governor-of-Arkansas biographical trajectory; the "Comeback Kid" 1992 Democratic primary survival; the "It's the Economy, Stupid" 1992 general-election victory over George H.W. Bush; the post-1994 "triangulation" against the Newt Gingrich House Republican majority; the 1996 re-election; the Lewinsky scandal and impeachment of 1998-1999; the 2000 Al Gore presidential candidacy that Clinton's post-impeachment political capital could not save; the post-Presidency Clinton Foundation and the multimillion-dollar speaking-fee portfolio that produced the family's commercial position. The record is what the political-class operator did with the access of the American Presidency across the eight years 1993-2001 and the commercial portfolio across 2001-2026. The biographical record is the public-facing presentation. The institutional record reveals the actual policy outputs.

## The 1992 election and the Third Way Democratic-party model

The 1992 Clinton campaign was constructed in opposition to the post-1972 Democratic-party model that George McGovern, Walter Mondale, and Michael Dukakis had operated. The Democratic Leadership Council, founded in 1985 by Al From, was the structural vehicle through which Clinton, Al Gore, and the southern-Democratic congressional faction (Sam Nunn; John Breaux) built the policy platform that became the Clinton 1992 campaign. The DLC's stated mission was to move the Democratic Party away from the post-1968 "liberal" identification with organised labour, civil rights, and anti-war positions toward a "centrist" position that the DLC argued could win back the working-class white voters who had moved to Reagan and Bush across 1968-1988.

The 1992 election produced Clinton's victory with 43.0% of the popular vote (370 Electoral College votes) against George H.W. Bush (37.5%, 168 EC) and Ross Perot (18.9%, 0 EC). The Perot third-party candidacy was the documented factor in the 1992 outcome; the post-1992 analysis has contested the counterfactual outcome of a two-way Clinton-Bush race, but the documented Perot vote split establishes that the 1992 outcome was the product of a three-way race in which Clinton won roughly 43% of the popular vote. The 1992 election was the first Democratic presidential victory since Jimmy Carter's 1976 win, and the first Democratic victory in the post-1968 Reagan-Bush settlement period.

The Clinton 1992 campaign delivered to the Democratic Party the policy framework that the subsequent Democratic-party operators would refine across the 1993-2016 period: free-trade-and-financial-deregulation policy from the centrist-Democratic-and-Wall-Street-aligned wing of the party; social-policy positions on abortion, gun control, and civil rights from the post-1968 liberal-Democratic wing; deficit-reduction-and-balanced-budget fiscal positions from the deficit-hawk wing; and rhetorical "third way" positioning between the post-1968 liberal-Democratic and the Reagan-Bush conservative-Republican models. The framework was the operating model that produced the 1996 Clinton re-election, the 1997-2001 federal-budget-surplus period, and the post-2001 American Democratic-party position that the Bush administration's 2003 Iraq invasion and the 2008 financial collapse subsequently destabilised.

## NAFTA — the 1993 North American Free Trade Agreement

The North American Free Trade Agreement was the post-Cold War trade-policy operation that established the post-1993 American manufacturing-employment trajectory. NAFTA was negotiated under the George H.W. Bush administration in 1992; Clinton's post-election position was to ratify the agreement with the labour-and-environmental side agreements that the 1993 congressional negotiations produced. Clinton signed NAFTA on 8 December 1993; the agreement took effect on 1 January 1994.

The outcome was contested across the thirty years. The US Chamber of Commerce position is that NAFTA increased US trade in goods and services with Canada and Mexico from $337 billion in 1993 to $1.2 trillion in 2011. The AFL-CIO position is that NAFTA caused roughly 700,000 American manufacturing jobs to shift to Mexico over the same period. The post-2000 academic-economic-analysis literature (Robert Scott of the Economic Policy Institute; Ian Fletcher; the post-2010 industrial-policy revival faction) has documented the NAFTA-and-WTO-accession-driven 1995-2010 American manufacturing-employment collapse as a structural cause of the post-2008 Rust Belt political-class realignment that produced the 2016 Trump campaign. The analytical conclusion is documentary: the trade-volume increase occurred; the manufacturing-employment shift occurred; the political consequence of the manufacturing-employment shift produced the post-2008 Rust Belt voter realignment that the 2016 Trump campaign successfully exploited.

The Clinton administration's parallel push for the World Trade Organisation accession of China across 2000-2001 — which the Clinton administration negotiated and the subsequent George W. Bush administration completed on 11 December 2001 — was the parallel operation that produced the post-2001 American manufacturing-employment collapse that the 2010-2016 critical-trade-policy academic class (David Autor; David Dorn; Gordon Hanson) documented as the "China shock" responsible for the loss of roughly 2-2.4 million American manufacturing jobs across 2001-2011.

## The 1994 Crime Bill

The Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994, signed by Clinton on 13 September 1994, was the signature criminal-justice legislative operation of the first Clinton term. The bill appropriated $30.2 billion across the 1994-2000 period including funding for 100,000 new police officers, the federal three-strikes provision (mandatory life imprisonment for third felony convictions), the federal death-penalty expansion (60 new federal capital offences), the Federal Assault Weapons Ban (1994-2004), and the Violence Against Women Act.

The Senate co-sponsor of the 1994 Crime Bill was Joe Biden, then the Democratic chairman of the Senate Judiciary Committee, whose role in the legislation is covered in Dossier 1.

The outcome was the post-1994 American mass-incarceration expansion. The 2008 federal prison population was roughly 2.3 million people, up from roughly 1 million in 1990 — the largest carceral population in absolute and per-capita terms in the world. The 1994 Crime Bill was the documented contributing factor in the 1990s-and-2000s American mass-incarceration expansion that produced the post-2010 racial-justice analytical class's documentation of the racialised carceral consequences (Michelle Alexander, *The New Jim Crow*, 2010; the Black Lives Matter movement from 2013).

Hillary Clinton publicly acknowledged in her 2016 primary campaign that the 1994 Crime Bill had produced unintended mass-incarceration consequences. Bill Clinton himself, in his April 2016 confrontation with Black Lives Matter protesters at a Philadelphia campaign rally for his wife, publicly defended the bill on the record before subsequently acknowledging the unintended consequences. The acknowledgment came twenty-two years after the bill was signed.

## Welfare Reform — the 1996 PRWORA

The Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996, signed by Clinton on 22 August 1996, replaced Aid to Families with Dependent Children — the federal entitlement programme established under Franklin Roosevelt's Social Security Act of 1935 — with Temporary Assistance for Needy Families, a block-grant programme administered by the states with five-year lifetime limits on benefits.

The 1996 Clinton administration's internal opposition included Department of Health and Human Services Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation Peter Edelman, who resigned in protest. Edelman's 1997 *Atlantic Monthly* essay, "The Worst Thing Bill Clinton Has Done," documented the post-PRWORA welfare-roll-and-poverty-outcome data and predicted the welfare-rolls-down, deep-poverty-up trajectory.

The outcome documented was the post-1996 reduction of welfare rolls by roughly 60% by 2000 per the administration accounting, alongside the documented post-1996 expansion of deep poverty — American households with under-$2-per-day cash incomes — to roughly 1.5 million households by 2011 per the Edin and Shaefer 2015 *$2.00 a Day: Living on Almost Nothing in America* documentation. The PRWORA achieved the welfare-rolls-reduction outcome that the centrist-Democratic-and-Republican coalition had targeted; the deep-poverty consequence was the documented downstream effect that the Edelman 1997 prediction had identified.

## The 1999 Glass-Steagall repeal and the 2000 Commodity Futures Modernization Act

The Financial Services Modernization Act of 1999 — the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act — signed by Clinton on 12 November 1999, repealed the 1933 Glass-Steagall Act provisions (Sections 20 and 32) that had separated commercial banking from investment banking and insurance since the post-1929-collapse banking reform of the early New Deal period. The repeal permitted bank-holding companies to combine commercial banking, investment banking, and insurance operations within a single corporate structure.

The legislation's pre-positioning was the 1998 Citicorp-Travelers Group merger that created Citigroup — the merger that had been permitted under the 1956 Bank Holding Company Act's five-year grace period but that required the 1999 GLBA to be permanently legalised. The 1998 merger was the position that pushed the 1999 legislation through Congress. The Treasury Secretary at the negotiation through October 1999 was Robert Rubin (Treasury Secretary 1995-1999), who left Treasury for Citigroup in October 1999 and received roughly $126 million in Citigroup compensation across the subsequent 2000-2008 period. Lawrence Summers succeeded Rubin as Treasury Secretary and signed the GLBA on Clinton's behalf.

Clinton's public statement on signing the GLBA (12 November 1999): "This is a day we can celebrate as an American day... The Glass-Steagall law is no longer appropriate for the economy in which we live... Today what we are doing is modernizing the financial services industry, tearing down these antiquated laws and granting banks significant new authority."

The post-2008 academic-finance analysis is documented. The Joseph Stiglitz position (Nobel Memorial Prize in Economics, 2001) is that the post-GLBA "investment-bank culture came out on top" in the merged commercial-and-investment-bank holding companies, with the consequence that the risk-taking culture of investment banking displaced the conservative culture of commercial banking. The Elizabeth Warren position is documented: Glass-Steagall had kept banks from doing "crazy things" and the FDIC insurance, the Glass-Steagall separation, and the SEC regulations had together provided "50 years without a crisis" before the GLBA. The alternative academic position (Alan Blinder; Melanie Fein; Ben Bernanke) is that the 2008 crisis was driven by shadow-banking-firm activities that the GLBA had not authorised. The operative conclusion is documentary: the GLBA was the 1999 deregulation that the major banking lobby had pursued for two decades; the 1998 Citigroup merger had pre-positioned the need for the repeal; the Treasury Secretary Rubin transitioned to Citigroup compensation immediately after the repeal; and the post-2008 academic position that GLBA was a contributing factor in the 2008 collapse is held by the critical-finance-academic class while the alternative position is held by post-2008 banking-defender academic figures.

The Commodity Futures Modernization Act of 2000, signed by Clinton on 21 December 2000, was the parallel late-Clinton deregulatory operation that exempted over-the-counter derivatives — including credit-default swaps and collateralised debt obligations — from CFTC and SEC regulatory oversight. The contemporaneous opposition came from Brooksley Born, the CFTC Chair from 1996 to 1999, who publicly warned across her 1997-1999 tenure that the unregulated OTC-derivatives market presented systemic risk. The Clinton administration's Treasury team (Robert Rubin; Lawrence Summers; Federal Reserve Chair Alan Greenspan) publicly opposed Born's regulatory proposals. Born resigned in June 1999 after the 1998 Long-Term Capital Management collapse — the pre-2008 warning of the systemic-risk profile of unregulated derivatives — produced no regulatory response.

The outcome was the unregulated OTC-derivatives market growing from roughly $80 trillion in notional value at signing to roughly $683 trillion at the 2008 collapse, per the Bank for International Settlements figures. The 2008 financial collapse was, on the subsequent FCIC (Financial Crisis Inquiry Commission) 2011 report findings, driven in significant part by the unregulated derivatives market that the 2000 CFMA had legalised.

The combined documentary record of the 1999 GLBA and the 2000 CFMA is that the late-Clinton-administration deregulatory operations created the governing conditions for the 2008 financial collapse. The Robert Rubin-to-Citigroup revolving door is the documented commercial-political arrangement between the Clinton-administration Treasury and the creditor banks that the deregulation produced. The Brooksley Born public-record warnings are the documented contemporaneous opposition that the Clinton-administration Treasury rejected. The arrangement is documented.

## The Lewinsky scandal and impeachment

The 1995-1997 Bill Clinton sexual relationship with Monica Lewinsky, a 21-year-old unpaid White House intern at the commencement of the relationship, became the subject of the 1998 Independent Counsel Kenneth Starr investigation when Linda Tripp (Lewinsky's Pentagon co-worker) provided FBI agents with recordings of conversations with Lewinsky describing the relationship.

On 17 January 1998, Clinton was deposed under oath in the Paula Jones sexual-harassment civil suit and denied a sexual relationship with Lewinsky. On 26 January 1998, Clinton made the televised public statement: "I did not have sexual relations with that woman, Miss Lewinsky." On 17 August 1998, Clinton testified before the federal grand jury in the Starr investigation and acknowledged "inappropriate" conduct with Lewinsky in a televised public statement, without admitting to perjury, instructing others to lie, or covering up. On 9 September 1998, Starr submitted his report to the House of Representatives alleging eleven grounds for impeachment including perjury, obstruction of justice, witness tampering, and abuse of power.

The House of Representatives voted on 19 December 1998 to impeach Clinton on two articles — Article I (perjury before the grand jury) and Article III (obstruction of justice). Articles II and IV (perjury in the Paula Jones civil deposition, and abuse of power) failed in the House. Clinton became the second American President impeached, the first being Andrew Johnson in 1868.

The Senate acquitted Clinton on both articles on 12 February 1999. On Article I (perjury): 45 not guilty (45 Democrats and 10 Republicans), 55 guilty (45 Republicans). On Article III (obstruction): 50 not guilty (45 Democrats and 5 Republicans), 50 guilty (50 Republicans). The 67-vote conviction threshold was not met on either article.

On 12 April 1999, Federal District Judge Susan Webber Wright cited Clinton for civil contempt of court for his "willful failure" to obey her order to testify truthfully in the Paula Jones deposition. The $90,000 fine was assessed; the matter was referred to the Arkansas Supreme Court for disciplinary action.

On 19 January 2001, the day before leaving office, Clinton entered an agreement with the Independent Counsel under which he admitted to false testimony, had his Arkansas law licence suspended for five years, and paid a $25,000 fine to the Arkansas Bar Association — in exchange for the Independent Counsel's agreement not to pursue post-Presidency criminal charges. The documentary record is that Clinton, having denied the perjury through the 1998-1999 impeachment proceedings, acknowledged the false testimony in the 19 January 2001 settlement at the moment the political risk of acknowledgment had been eliminated by the impending end of his presidency.

## The Marc Rich pardon

On Clinton's last day in office, 20 January 2001, he issued 140 pardons and 36 commutations — the largest single-day pardon-and-commutation total of his presidency. The pardon of Marc Rich was the controversy that defined the public response.

Marc Rich, a commodities trader, had been indicted in 1983 on 65 counts including systematic tax fraud evading more than $48 million in tax liability, wire fraud, racketeering, and illegal oil-trading transactions with Iran during the 1979-1980 hostage crisis. Rich fled the United States immediately after the indictment and remained in Switzerland for the subsequent 18 years on the FBI Ten Most Wanted list. Clinton pardoned Rich on the 20 January 2001 last-day pardon list.

The pre-pardon donations from Denise Eisenberg Rich, Marc Rich's former wife, totalled roughly $450,000 to the Clinton Presidential Library Foundation, over $1 million to Democratic Party campaigns across the 1990s, and contributions to Hillary Clinton's 2000 Senate campaign across the pre-pardon period. The pardon procedure bypassed the Department of Justice Office of the Pardon Attorney, the conventional procedural route. The internal Clinton administration record (subsequent congressional testimony by John Podesta) was that White House aides including Podesta had urged Clinton not to grant the pardon.

The 2001-2005 federal investigation into the pardon was opened by Manhatta US Attorney Mary Jo White in 2001, transferred to her successor James Comey — the same Comey who, fifteen years later, would send the 28 October 2016 letter to Congress that figures in Dossier 4 — and closed in 2005 without charges. Clinton's subsequent public acknowledgment was that the Rich pardon was "terrible politics" and "wasn't worth the damage to my reputation."

The Rich pardon was the central pardon controversy of the post-1974 American Presidential pardon record. The pattern — a major Democratic-Party donor's relative receives a pardon at the moment of the President's outgoing-administration vulnerability, with the pardon procedure bypassing the conventional Justice Department vetting — was the documented commercial-political arrangement that the commercial press treated as anomalous rather than as the structural pattern of the post-1968 American Presidential pardon system that the subsequent Bush, Obama, and Trump presidencies would extend. The arrangement is documented.

## The post-Presidency commercial portfolio

### Speaking fees

The post-Presidency Bill Clinton speaking-fee record across 2001-2015 totalled roughly $105 million across roughly 542 paid speeches per the 2015 *Washington Post* analysis of the Hillary Clinton federal financial-disclosure forms. The standard Clinton speaking fee across the 2001-2015 period was in the $200,000-$500,000 range per appearance, with major corporate clients paying the higher end of the range.

The largest single-corporate-source speaking-fee total was $1.5 million from UBS, the Swiss bank, across the 2011-2014 question-and-answer-series sessions with UBS Wealth Management CEO Bob McCann. The UBS arrangement is on the 2009 IRS-settlement record covered in Dossier 4. The sequence — Hillary Clinton brokers the 2009 UBS-IRS settlement as Secretary of State, UBS donations to the Clinton Foundation grow from under $60,000 through 2008 to roughly $600,000 by end of 2014, UBS pays Bill Clinton $1.5 million across 2011-2014 — is the documented commercial-political arrangement between the Clinton family and the multinational bank whose IRS investigation Hillary Clinton had brokered the settlement of.

The 2001-2015 speaking-fee record was the post-Presidency commercial-political portfolio that built the Clinton family financial position from the "we were dead broke" 2000 public statement Hillary Clinton made about the post-White House financial position to the estimated $200-million-plus Clinton family net worth by 2015.

### The Clinton Foundation

The Clinton Foundation was founded 2001 as the William J. Clinton Foundation; renamed the Bill, Hillary & Chelsea Clinton Foundation in 2013. The Foundation operated global-public-health programmes through the Clinton Health Access Initiative (CHAI) and global-development programmes through the Clinton Global Initiative (founded 2005). The 2009-2015 foreign-donation portfolio that produced the documented commercial-political arrangements with foreign governments during Hillary Clinton's Secretary of State tenure is covered in Dossier 4.

The 2015 *Clinton Cash* reporting by Peter Schweizer documented the pattern of commercial-political arrangements between Clinton Foundation donors and the US State Department under Hillary Clinton's tenure. The pattern — Foundation donations from foreign-government and private-sector donors coinciding with favourable State Department decisions affecting the donors — was the documented commercial-political arrangement that the subsequent 2016 election-cycle press coverage of the Foundation built on.

### The Jeffrey Epstein relationship

The post-Presidency Bill Clinton relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, the American financier and convicted child-sex offender, is documented across the 2002-2003 period.

The FAA flight logs for Epstein's Boeing 727 jet (registration N908JE, the aircraft media-nicknamed the "Lolita Express") document Bill Clinton as a passenger on at least 26 flight segments across the 2002-2003 period per the 6 May 2016 Fox News reporting on the court-released flight logs. The Wikipedia "Lolita Express" entry cites 27 flights to "at least a dozen international locations" across the same period. In September 2002, Clinton flew on Epstein's aircraft to Africa with actors Kevin Spacey and Chris Tucker on an HIV/AIDS-project tour.

The 2017 Freedom of Information Act request established that there was no Secret Service documentary record of Clinton visiting Epstein's Little St. James island in the US Virgin Islands. The Epstein flight logs did not document Clinton flights to the island. The 2020 *Vanity Fair* reporting cited former Clinton advisor Doug Band as saying that Clinton had visited the island in January 2003; a Clinton spokesperson denied the visit and provided travel logs that did not include any mention of it. The structural conclusion: the documented flight-log record of the 26-27 Epstein-aircraft flights is the evidentiary baseline; the contested island-visit record is on the post-2019 record subject to ongoing contestation.

Clinton's 2019 spokesperson Angel Ureña stated that Clinton had "not spoken to Epstein in well over a decade, and has never been to Little Saint James, Epstein's ranch in New Mexico, or his residence in Florida." The statement reduced the Clinton acknowledgment of the Epstein flights to "four trips" on the aircraft — the count that requires aggregating multiple flight segments into single "trips" to reach the four-trip figure rather than the 26-27 flight-segment figure that the documented flight logs record.

In August 2025, the House Oversight Committee under Chairman James Comer issued subpoenas for testimony from Bill Clinton and Hillary Clinton regarding their Epstein interactions, along with a request for the "full, complete, unredacted Epstein Files." The subpoenas have been the subject of contested compliance through the 2025-2026 period and have not, on the 2026 public record, produced public testimony from Bill or Hillary Clinton.

## The upshot

William Jefferson Clinton was, across the eight years of the Clinton presidency and the twenty-five-year post-Presidency commercial portfolio, the post-1968 American Democratic-Party operator who built the Third Way Democratic-party model that the post-Clinton Democratic-Party operators — Tony Blair in Britain, Gerhard Schröder in Germany, Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton and Joe Biden in the United States — would subsequently operate. The record across the eight years 1993-2001 had three threads.

Economic-policy and deregulation: the 1993 NAFTA that produced the post-1995 American manufacturing-employment shift to Mexico that the AFL-CIO estimated at 700,000 jobs; the 1999 GLBA repeal of Glass-Steagall that produced the 1998-2008 commercial-and-investment-banking integration that the 2008 collapse exposed; and the 2000 CFMA that legalised the unregulated OTC-derivatives market that grew to $683 trillion in notional value by 2008.

Crime, welfare, and incarceration: the 1994 Crime Bill that produced the post-1994 American mass-incarceration expansion; and the 1996 PRWORA welfare reform that reduced welfare rolls by 60% and expanded deep poverty to 1.5 million American households by 2011.

Impeachment and pardon: the 1995-1997 Lewinsky relationship that produced the 1998 impeachment and the 19 January 2001 false-testimony acknowledgment; and the 20 January 2001 Marc Rich pardon that defined the post-Presidency pardon-controversy record. The record throughout the post-Presidency twenty-five years 2001-2026 was: the $105 million in speaking fees across 542 paid speeches; the Clinton Foundation foreign-donation portfolio that produced the documented commercial-political arrangements with foreign governments during Hillary Clinton's Secretary of State tenure; the documented 26-27 Epstein-aircraft flights across 2002-2003; and the 2025 House Oversight Committee subpoenas that the Clintons have, on the 2026 public record, not yet publicly testified to.

The Rhodes Scholar repealed Glass-Steagall, signed the Crime Bill, flew on the Epstein aircraft twenty-six times, and collected $105 million in speaking fees. The philanthropy presentation continued uninterrupted.
