# Sources — Dossier 2: Donald J. Trump

## Biographical
- Born 14 June 1946, Queens, New York City.
- Son of Fred Trump (1905-1999), the post-war New York City outer-borough residential real-estate developer, and Mary Anne MacLeod Trump (1912-2000), Scottish immigrant.
- Brothers: Fred Jr. (1938-1981, died from alcoholism), Robert (1948-2020); sisters: Maryanne Trump Barry (1937-2023, US Court of Appeals judge), Elizabeth Trump Grau.
- New York Military Academy 1959-1964; Fordham University 1964-1966; Wharton School of Business (BS in economics) 1966-1968.
- Five Vietnam War draft deferments (four educational, one medical for "bone spurs" in the heels — the bone-spurs diagnosis was provided by Dr Larry Braunstein, a Queens podiatrist who was a tenant of Fred Trump in the early 1970s; documented in *New York Times*, December 2018).
- Married Ivana Zelníčková 1977 (divorced 1992); Marla Maples 1993 (divorced 1999); Melania Knauss 2005.
- Children: Donald Jr. (b. 1977), Ivanka (b. 1981), Eric (b. 1984), Tiffany (b. 1993), Barron (b. 2006).

## Inheritance and the Fred Trump fortune

### The 2018 New York Times investigation
- The 2018 New York Times investigation by David Barstow, Susanne Craig, and Russ Buettner (published 2 October 2018, drawing on over 100,000 pages of confidential tax returns and family financial records from a confidential source) established that Donald Trump received the present-day equivalent of $413 million from his father Fred Trump's real estate empire across the course of his life.
- The transfers began in 1949, when Donald was 3 years old; by age 3, Donald Trump was receiving the equivalent of $200,000 per year (in 2018 dollars) from his father's real-estate empire.
- Fred Trump made Donald Trump a millionaire by the time Donald was 8.
- Total transfers from Fred and Mary Trump to their five children: over $1 billion in 2018 dollars.
- Tax structuring: the Times investigation documented that Fred Trump and family used a series of mechanisms — trusts established when Donald was a child, below-market property transfers, the 1992 "All County Building Supply & Maintenance" subsidiary set up to funnel Fred Trump's fortune to his children, undervaluation of properties for tax purposes — to avoid an estimated $500+ million in gift and estate taxes.
- The Times reported that properties sold by the four surviving Trump children in 2004 (to an investment group led by Rubin Schron for $600 million) were valued in the family's earlier filings at over 16 times less than their actual market value.
- New York magazine (Jonathan Chait, 2018): "many of Fred's contributions to Donald were by definition criminal in nature."
- The 2018 leaked tax returns showed Donald Trump's 2005 income at $153 million.
- December 1990: Donald Trump attempted to amend his father's will. According to Donald's sister Maryanne Trump Barry's later legal testimony, the amendment "was basically taking the whole estate and giving it to Donald", allowing him to "sell, do anything he wants... with the properties". *Washington Post* documented the proposed amendment "was designed to protect Donald Trump's inheritance from efforts to seize it by creditors and Ivana", whom he divorced that month. Fred rejected the proposal.

### Sources
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fred_Trump)
- [NPR Fresh Air, "Times Journalists Puncture Myth Of Trump As Self-Made Billionaire," 18 October 2018](https://www.npr.org/2018/10/18/658442355/times-journalists-puncture-myth-of-trump-as-self-made-billionaire)
- *New York Times*, "Trump Engaged in Suspect Tax Schemes as He Reaped Riches From His Father," 2 October 2018 (Barstow, Craig, Buettner).
- [Factually.co](https://factually.co/fact-checks/politics/donald-trump-inheritance-value-from-fred-trump-estate-0b41e4)
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump)
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

## Manhattan real estate career
- **1971:** Donald Trump became president of his father's real-estate business; renamed it the Trump Organization.
- **1976-1980:** Grand Hyatt Hotel construction (the former Commodore Hotel adjacent to Grand Central Terminal); first major Manhattan project; financed through a 40-year tax abatement from New York City worth $400 million across the abatement period — the largest tax abatement ever granted to a private developer in New York City history at the time.
- **1983:** Trump Tower opens at 725 Fifth Avenue. 58-floor mixed-use building. Built through the deliberate destruction of the Art Deco friezes on the Bonwit Teller building, which Trump had committed to preserving for the Metropolitan Museum of Art and then ordered destroyed by undocumented Polish labourers (the "Polish Brigade" case, *In re Diduck v. Kaszycki & Sons*, settled in 1999 with Trump paying $1.375 million).
- **1985:** purchased Mar-a-Lago in Palm Beach, Florida from Marjorie Merriweather Post estate for $7-10 million.
- **1985-1988:** Atlantic City casino acquisitions and Plaza Hotel acquisition.

## Atlantic City casinos
- **1982 (15 March):** New Jersey Casino Control Commission grants Donald Trump a casino licence.
- **1984:** Trump Plaza Hotel & Casino opens.
- **1985:** Trump Castle (later Trump Marina) acquired.
- **1988:** Trump purchases the unfinished Trump Taj Mahal property from Resorts International for $230 million through negotiations with Merv Griffin.
- **1990 (April):** Trump Taj Mahal opens at a total construction cost of $1 billion, financed through junk bonds at 14% interest.
- **1989 (10 October):** Helicopter crash in northern New Jersey kills three Trump casino executives — Stephen F. Hyde (CEO of Trump's casino operations), Mark Grossinger Etess (President and COO of the Taj Mahal), and Jonathan Benanav (Trump Plaza EVP).

### The four corporate bankruptcies
- **Bankruptcy 1: Trump Taj Mahal Associates (1991).** Chapter 11 filed within a year of the Taj's opening; Trump Taj Mahal Associates was burdened with $1 billion in junk-bond debt at 14% interest in a recessionary gambling market. In the restructuring, Trump gave up 50% of the Taj Mahal property in exchange for reduced debt service; bondholders accepted new debt instruments worth less than the original bonds.
- **Bankruptcy 2: Trump Plaza Associates and related entities (1992).** Trump Plaza, Trump Castle, and the Plaza Hotel in Manhattan filed for Chapter 11 protection in 1992 with combined debt over $1 billion. Trump gave up 49% of the Plaza Hotel to a group of six lenders in exchange for restructured debt terms.
- **Bankruptcy 3: Trump Hotels & Casino Resorts (2004).** The umbrella public company (NYSE: DJT, after Trump's initials) filed for Chapter 11 with $1.8 billion in debt. The company was renamed Trump Entertainment Resorts. Trump's ownership stake was reduced from 56% to 28%.
- **Bankruptcy 4: Trump Entertainment Resorts (2009).** Second parent-level Chapter 11 with $500 million in debt; Trump resigned as chairman; his ownership stake reduced to 10%.
- **Subsequent: Trump Entertainment Resorts (2014).** Third Chapter 11 of the holding company (some counts list this as a fifth Trump-tied bankruptcy); Trump sued for removal of his name from the casinos.
- **2016:** Trump Entertainment Resorts became a subsidiary of Icahn Enterprises.
- **Aftermath:** all Trump-branded Atlantic City casino properties were closed or sold by 2017. Trump Plaza was demolished in 2021.

### Contractor non-payment pattern
- Across the 1980s and 1990s, Atlantic City contractors developed the established practice of bidding 10-20% above their target price on Trump projects on the assumption that Trump would pay only cents on the dollar. Multiple documented cases: J. Michael Diehl (Freehold Music Center, $100,000 piano sale, paid pennies on the dollar); the subcontractors on the Taj Mahal construction (over 200 subcontractors collectively owed $69 million at the time of the 1991 bankruptcy, receiving cents on the dollar through the bankruptcy reorganisation).

### Sources
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trump_Entertainment_Resorts)
- [*Newsweek*, "Donald Trump and the Rise and Fall of Atlantic City," 30 October 2015](https://www.newsweek.com/2015/10/30/trumps-big-atlantic-city-dump-385427.html)
- [American Presidency Project](https://www.presidency.ucsb.edu/documents/rubio-campaign-press-release-the-four-times-donald-trump-has-declared-bankruptcy)
- [NBC News, "Trump casinos file for bankruptcy," 22 November 2004](https://www.nbcnews.com/id/wbna6556470)
- [Factually.co](https://factually.co/fact-checks/business/trump-casino-bankruptcies-years-count-310f2d)
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

## *The Apprentice* and brand period (2004-2015)
- **2004 (January):** *The Apprentice* premieres on NBC. Mark Burnett Productions; Trump as host and executive producer.
- **Earnings:** by Trump's own 2015 disclosures, *The Apprentice* franchise paid Trump $213 million across 2004-2015 in salary, executive producer fees, and licensing royalties.
- **Brand licensing:** the 2004-2015 period was Trump's principal income period through brand-licensing arrangements rather than active real-estate development. Trump-branded properties licensed (but not owned) by the Trump Organization included: Trump SoHo (New York, 2010); Trump International Hotel and Tower Toronto (2012); Trump Vancouver (2017); Trump branded products including Trump Steaks, Trump Vodka, Trump University, Trump magazine, Trump Mortgage, Trump Network multi-level marketing scheme. Most of the consumer-product ventures failed within five years of launch.
- **Trump University (2005-2010):** for-profit real-estate seminar business marketed as a university; subject to fraud lawsuits brought by the New York State Attorney General Eric Schneiderman (2013) and class-action plaintiffs in California. Trump settled the lawsuits in November 2016, after his election but before his inauguration, for $25 million. The settlement was completed in 2018.

## First presidential campaign and presidency (2015-2021)
- **2015 (16 June):** announces 2016 presidential campaign at Trump Tower.
- **2016 (8 November):** elected 45th President of the United States, defeating Hillary Clinton with 304 Electoral College votes to 227, while losing the popular vote by 2.9 million.
- **2017 (20 January):** inaugurated as 45th President.
- **First-term legislation:** Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of December 2017 (the principal first-term legislative achievement; reduced corporate tax rate from 35% to 21%; documented in standard post-2017 fiscal-policy analysis as having delivered the principal benefit to the American corporate-and-investor class).
- **First-term Supreme Court nominations:** Neil Gorsuch (2017, replacing Antonin Scalia), Brett Kavanaugh (2018, replacing Anthony Kennedy), Amy Coney Barrett (2020, replacing Ruth Bader Ginsburg). The three nominations, all confirmed, gave the Court a 6-3 conservative majority that produced *Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization* in June 2022, overturning *Roe v. Wade*.
- **First impeachment (December 2019 - February 2020):** House impeached Trump for abuse of power and obstruction of Congress regarding the 2019 phone call with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in which Trump pressured Zelensky to investigate the Burisma arrangement (Dossier 1 above). Senate acquitted along party lines.
- **COVID-19 pandemic response (2020-2021):** the documented response failures across early 2020, including the public statements by Trump downplaying the virus contradicting his contemporaneous private statements to Bob Woodward (the documented "I wanted to always play it down" recordings of Trump-Woodward conversations in March 2020).
- **2020 (3 November):** loses presidential election to Joe Biden, 232 Electoral College votes to 306; loses popular vote by 7 million.
- **2020-2021 post-election period:** the documented sequence of legal challenges to the election results — over 60 court filings, all of which were rejected (including by judges appointed by Trump); the documented 2 January 2021 phone call to Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger ("I just want to find 11,780 votes"); the documented 6 January 2021 rally near the White House at which Trump told the crowd to "fight like hell" before the crowd marched to the Capitol; the subsequent events of 6 January 2021 in which 2,000 of his supporters breached the Capitol building, five people died in the immediate aftermath, four Capitol Police officers died by suicide in the months following, and the certification of Joe Biden's election was delayed by six hours.
- **Second impeachment (January-February 2021):** House impeached Trump for incitement of insurrection. Senate acquitted 57-43 (10 votes short of conviction); the 57 votes for conviction included seven Republican senators.

## Post-presidency criminal and civil legal record (2021-2024)

### Civil cases

#### E. Jean Carroll (Carroll II, May 2023)
- **9 May 2023:** A federal civil jury in the US District Court for the Southern District of New York found Donald Trump liable for sexual abuse and defamation of E. Jean Carroll. The jury found, by preponderance of the evidence (the civil standard), that Trump had sexually abused Carroll at a Bergdorf Goodman department store dressing room in Manhattan in spring 1996. The judge, Lewis Kaplan, subsequently clarified that the jury's finding of "sexual abuse" under New York law was equivalent to "rape" in the common everyday meaning of the term (i.e., not requiring penile penetration under the specific New York Penal Law definition).
- **Damages awarded:** $5 million total — $2 million compensatory for sexual battery, $2.7 million compensatory for defamation, $280,000 punitive damages.
- **Appeal:** the Second Circuit Court of Appeals upheld the $5 million award on 30 December 2024.

#### E. Jean Carroll (Carroll I defamation, January 2024)
- **26 January 2024:** A federal civil jury found Trump liable for defamation regarding his 2019 statements about Carroll and awarded $83.3 million in additional damages.
- **Appeal:** Second Circuit rejected Trump's appeal in April 2026 (with Trump's team indicating it would seek US Supreme Court review under the Westfall Act).

### Criminal cases

#### New York v. Trump (the Manhattan "hush money" case)
- **30 March 2023:** Manhattan grand jury indicts Trump on 34 felony counts of falsifying business records in the first degree (New York Penal Law § 175.10), the first criminal indictment of a former US president in American history. The case involved the $130,000 payment from Trump's lawyer Michael Cohen to adult-film actress Stormy Daniels in October 2016, made to prevent Daniels from publicly disclosing her allegation of a 2006 sexual encounter with Trump, and Trump's subsequent recording of his reimbursement payments to Cohen as "legal expenses" rather than the campaign-finance violations that the payments factually constituted.
- **30 May 2024:** A New York County jury convicted Trump on all 34 felony counts after nine and a half hours of deliberation following a six-week trial. First criminal conviction of a former US president in American history.
- **10 January 2025:** Judge Juan Merchan sentenced Trump to an "unconditional discharge" — the lightest possible sentence available under New York state law — citing the constitutional impracticality of imposing a meaningful penalty on a President-elect ten days before inauguration. Trump remains a convicted felon under New York law. Trump filed a formal appeal of the conviction in October 2025.

#### United States v. Trump (federal "classified documents" / Mar-a-Lago case)
- **8 June 2023:** Special Counsel Jack Smith files federal indictment in the Southern District of Florida (40 counts in the superseding indictment), charging Trump with retention of national defense information under the Espionage Act and obstruction of justice for his post-presidency retention of classified documents at Mar-a-Lago and his subsequent obstruction of federal recovery efforts.
- **15 July 2024:** US District Judge Aileen Cannon dismissed the case on the grounds that Special Counsel Smith had been unlawfully appointed; the dismissal was on appeal at the Eleventh Circuit when Trump won the November 2024 election; the Justice Department under Smith subsequently moved to dismiss the case with prejudice consistent with longstanding Justice Department policy against the prosecution of a sitting president.

#### United States v. Trump (federal "January 6" / DC case)
- **1 August 2023:** Special Counsel Jack Smith files federal indictment in the District of Columbia (four counts: conspiracy to defraud the United States, conspiracy to obstruct an official proceeding, obstruction of an official proceeding, conspiracy against rights), charging Trump with the conspiracy to overturn the 2020 election results.
- **1 July 2024:** US Supreme Court issued *Trump v. United States* (603 US 593), the immunity decision holding that former presidents have absolute immunity for "official acts" and presumptive immunity for "all official conduct." The decision was decided 6-3 along the conservative-liberal axis; Justice Sotomayor in dissent: "the President is now a king above the law." The decision narrowed the indictable conduct in the January 6 case.
- **November 2024 - January 2025:** following Trump's November 2024 election victory, Smith filed a motion to dismiss the case without prejudice, consistent with DOJ policy.

#### Georgia v. Trump (Fulton County RICO case)
- **14 August 2023:** Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis files state racketeering indictment against Trump and 18 co-defendants in connection with the post-2020 election conduct in Georgia, including the 2 January 2021 phone call to Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger.
- **December 2024:** Georgia Court of Appeals removed DA Fani Willis from the case over the documented romantic relationship with special prosecutor Nathan Wade; the case remains in legal limbo as of the time of this volume's writing.

### Civil fraud judgment (New York v. Trump Organization)

#### *People of the State of New York v. Trump et al.*
- **20 September 2022:** New York Attorney General Letitia James files civil fraud lawsuit against Donald Trump, his sons Donald Jr. and Eric Trump, his daughter Ivanka Trump (later dismissed from the case), Trump Organization entities, and Trump Organization executives Allen Weisselberg and Jeffrey McConney, alleging persistent fraud in business dealings under New York Executive Law § 63(12). The allegation: Trump and his organisation had falsified financial statements across the period 2014-2021, overvaluing assets by between $812 million and $2.2 billion across the period, in order to obtain favourable loan and insurance terms from banks and insurers.
- **26 September 2023:** Judge Arthur Engoron grants partial summary judgment for the state, finding that Trump and the co-defendants had committed fraud as a matter of law.
- **2 October 2023 - 11 January 2024:** trial held without a jury (bench trial) before Judge Engoron.
- **16 February 2024:** Judge Engoron rules against Trump, ordering disgorgement of $354,868,768 plus pre-judgment interest of $98.6 million (total Trump liability $453.5 million). Donald Jr. and Eric Trump fined $4 million each. Trump barred from serving as officer or director of any New York company for three years. Trump Organization barred from applying for loans from any New York bank for three years. Independent monitor (retired federal judge Barbara Jones) appointed for three years. Engoron's opinion: the frauds "shock the conscience"; the defendants' "complete lack of contrition and remorse borders on pathological."
- **August 2025:** A New York intermediate appellate court (Appellate Division, First Department) voided the disgorgement penalty on the grounds that the penalty was constitutionally excessive under the Eighth Amendment proportionality standard, while preserving the underlying findings of fraud. The bar on Trump serving as officer or director of New York companies was preserved.

### Sources
- [New York Attorney General's office press release](https://ag.ny.gov/press-release/2024/attorney-general-james-wins-landmark-victory-case-against-donald-trump)
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_business_fraud_lawsuit_against_the_Trump_Organization)
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E._Jean_Carroll_v._Donald_J._Trump)
- [PBS NewsHour, "Trump was sentenced to an 'unconditional discharge'," 10 January 2025](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/trump-was-sentenced-to-an-unconditional-discharge-in-his-hush-money-conviction-heres-what-that-means)
- [Washington Post](https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/02/25/trump-354-million-penalty-judgment/)
- [NPR, "Here's what all 34 felony counts in Trump's hush money trial mean," 30 May 2024](https://www.npr.org/2024/05/30/g-s1-1848/trump-hush-money-trial-34-counts)
- FindLaw, *Carroll v. [Trump](https://caselaw.findlaw.com/court/us-dis-crt-sd-new-yor/114642632.html)
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

## Second presidency (2025-present)
- **5 November 2024:** elected 47th President of the United States, defeating Vice President Kamala Harris with 312 Electoral College votes to 226; won popular vote by 2.3 million; only second US president (after Grover Cleveland) to serve non-consecutive terms.
- **20 January 2025:** inaugurated as 47th President.
- **The Riviera proposal:** in February 2025, Trump publicly proposed the redevelopment of the Gaza Strip into "the Riviera of the Middle East" through the displacement of the resident Palestinian population to Egypt and Jordan. The proposal is documented of Trump's joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House on 4 February 2025. The proposal was reaffirmed across multiple subsequent statements; the "Gaza Great Trust" arrangement involving Tony Blair's advisory role, Saudi Arabian sovereign-wealth involvement, and the documented sovereign-wealth-fund participation is covered in the Prologue of this volume.
- **The Smart Zone arrangement:** the documented February-March 2025 negotiation between Trump's advisers and Elon Musk for the construction of an autonomous "smart zone" in the Greenland-or-Canadian Arctic territory; covered in the Prologue.
- **Personnel:** the second-term Cabinet and White House staff staffed from the post-2020 Trump-aligned political-class network — including Pam Bondi as Attorney General, Marco Rubio as Secretary of State, Pete Hegseth as Secretary of Defense, Kristi Noem as Secretary of Homeland Security, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as Secretary of Health and Human Services, Tulsi Gabbard as Director of National Intelligence.
- **Tariff policy and trade war (March-October 2025):** the March-September 2025 imposition of broad tariffs on imports from major trading partners (China, the European Union, Canada, Mexico) at rates above the pre-2025 trade-policy baseline; documented disruption to global supply chains; documented inflationary pressure on US consumer prices across mid-2025.
- **Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE):** the January-July 2025 advisory body co-headed by Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy (Ramaswamy departed early 2025; Musk continued through July 2025 before exiting after public conflict with Trump); the documented post-DOGE federal workforce reductions and reorganisations.

### Sources
- Multiple post-inauguration press coverage 2025-2026 (BBC, *New York Times*, *Washington Post*, Reuters).
- The Prologue of this volume covers Riviera and Smart Zone in detail with primary-source citations.
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

## Cross-references
- Prologue of this volume: Trump Riviera proposal, Trump Smart Zone arrangement with Musk.

# Sources — Dossier 3: Barack Hussein Obama II

## Biographical
- Born 4 August 1961, Honolulu, Hawaii.
- Father: Barack Hussein Obama Sr. (1936-1982), Kenyan economist; mother: Stanley Ann Dunham (1942-1995), American anthropologist.
- Father and mother separated 1963, divorced 1964.
- Mother married Lolo Soetoro 1965; family moved to Jakarta, Indonesia 1967.
- Obama returned to Honolulu 1971 to live with his maternal grandparents Stanley and Madelyn Dunham; attended Punahou School 1971-1979.
- Occidental College 1979-1981; transferred to Columbia University 1981-1983; took BA in political science with specialty in international relations.
- New York community-organising work 1983-1985; Chicago community-organising work 1985-1988.
- Harvard Law School 1988-1991; elected first African-American president of the *Harvard Law Review* February 1990; took JD magna cum laude 1991.
- Married Michelle Robinson (Princeton AB 1985, Harvard Law JD 1988) 3 October 1992; daughters Malia (b. 1998) and Sasha (b. 2001).
- Practising lawyer in Chicago and lecturer at University of Chicago Law School 1992-2004.
- Illinois State Senate 1997-2004 (representing the 13th district, south-side Chicago).
- United States Senate 2005-2008 (junior senator from Illinois).
- 44th President of the United States 20 January 2009 - 20 January 2017.

## 2008 presidential campaign and Wall Street financing
- **2007-2008 campaign fundraising:** Obama raised $745 million across the 2008 campaign cycle — the largest American presidential campaign fundraising total in history at the time. Of this, $17.3 million came from the "securities and investment" sector (Center for Responsive Politics / OpenSecrets categorisation), the largest Wall Street fundraising total for any presidential candidate up to that point. Republican opponent John McCain raised $9.7 million from the same sector.
- **Goldman Sachs contributions:** Goldman Sachs employees and the Goldman Sachs PAC contributed $994,795 to Obama's 2008 campaign — the second-largest corporate-employee total after the University of California public-employee total. The Goldman Sachs total was over four times the $230,095 the firm contributed to McCain.
- **2008 banking-sector breakdown** (per OpenSecrets, the World Socialist Web Site analysis, Motley Fool aggregation):
- Securities and investment firms: $15 million
- Commercial banks: $3 million
- Other financial institutions: $6 million
- **2008 Goldman/JPMorgan/Citigroup combined:** Goldman Sachs ($994,795), JPMorgan Chase, and Citigroup combined contributed over $2.5 million to Obama's 2008 campaign. By contrast, McCain's top three Wall Street firms (Merrill Lynch, JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup) combined contributed $1.5 million.
- **2008 PolitiFact ruling on Wall Street fundraising:** "Mostly True" — Hillary Clinton's 2016 statement that "President Obama took more money from Wall Street in the 2008 campaign than anybody ever had" was rated Mostly True using the "securities and investment" sector measure.

### Sources
- [CNN, "Goldman Sachs was top Obama donor," 20 April 2010](https://www.cnn.com/2010/POLITICS/04/20/obama.goldman.donations/index.html)

## The post-2008 financial-crisis response

### Treasury staffing
The Obama Treasury, under Secretary Timothy Geithner (sworn in 26 January 2009 — confirmed despite the disclosure that he had failed to pay self-employment taxes 2001-2004 on his IMF income, totalling $34,023 plus interest), was staffed by figures with Goldman Sachs backgrounds or close working relationships:
- Mark Patterson, Geithner's Chief of Staff at Treasury — a registered Goldman Sachs lobbyist until 11 April 2008.
- Eileen Rominger, Director of Investment Management at the SEC — former Global Chief Investment Officer of Goldman Sachs Asset Management.
- Larry Summers, Director of the National Economic Council 2009-2010 — former Treasury Secretary under Clinton and an architect of the 1999 Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act repeal of Glass-Steagall, which had separated commercial and investment banking; Summers received $5.2 million in compensation from the D.E. Shaw hedge fund in 2008 alone.
- Rahm Emanuel, White House Chief of Staff 2009-2010 — former banker at Wasserstein Perella; Emanuel had earned over $18 million in 2.5 years as an investment banker.
- Robert D. Hormats, Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs — Vice Chairman of Goldman Sachs International.
- Diana Farrell, Deputy Director of the National Economic Council — Director of McKinsey Global Institute.
- Gene Sperling, Director of the National Economic Council 2011-2014 — former Goldman Sachs Group consultant 2008.
- Gary Gensler, Chairman of the CFTC 2009-2014 — 18-year Goldman Sachs partner.

### TARP and the bailout
The TARP bailout — the Troubled Asset Relief Program enacted under Bush in October 2008 and inherited by the Obama administration in January 2009 — disbursed $700 billion in emergency lending to the American financial system. The Obama administration's Treasury team continued the Bush-era policy of recapitalising the creditor banks without prosecuting the fraud cases the Justice Department had pending against the creditor banks at the time of the 2008 collapse. By the end of the Obama presidency, no executive of any of the systemically important American financial institutions had been criminally prosecuted for any conduct related to the 2008 financial collapse. The post-presidency-academic-analysis conclusion (Jeff Connaughton's *The Payoff*, 2012; Neil Barofsky's *Bailout*, 2012; the anti-trust faction of the American Economic Liberties Project) was that the failure to prosecute was a deliberate Obama Treasury choice rather than a constraint imposed by the facts of the cases.

### HAMP — the Home Affordable Modification Program
- **Announced:** 18 February 2009 by President Obama in Mesa, Arizona.
- **Promise:** to assist 3 to 4 million homeowners modify their mortgages to avoid foreclosure.
- **Authorised funding:** $50 billion from the TARP allocation, supplemented by $25 billion from Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
- **Outcome by end of Obama presidency:** under 1 million homeowners received ongoing assistance; nearly one in three of those receiving modifications re-defaulted after receiving inadequate modifications; 6 million American families lost their homes to foreclosure across the period 2008-2016.
- **Spent:** $4 billion of the original $50 billion HAMP budget by 2012, never reaching even 10% utilisation by program end.
- ** Treasury statement to the Congressional Oversight Panel** (Geithner to Elizabeth Warren, 2009, per Neil Barofsky's documented account in *Bailout: An Inside Account of How Washington Abandoned Main Street While Rescuing Wall Street*, 2012): "We estimate that they [the banks] can handle ten million foreclosures, over time. This program will help foam the runway for them." The public statement that the program's documented purpose was to protect bank balance sheets rather than homeowners is on the record of Barofsky's book and Warren's contemporaneous account.
- **Servicer misconduct:** in the documented class-action lawsuit *In re Bank of America Home Affordable Modification Program Litigation*, Bank of America's mortgage-servicing employees testified that they were told to lie to homeowners, deliberately misplace their documents, and deny loan modifications without explanation; managers rewarded them with bonuses (Target gift cards) for pushing borrowers into foreclosure. The Treasury department, documented, never permanently sanctioned a single mortgage servicer for HAMP violations by clawing back the incentive payments.
- **The cramdown amendment** — a proposed amendment to the bankruptcy code that would have allowed bankruptcy judges to modify mortgage terms (analogous to the 1934 Frazier-Lemke Act for farmers) — failed in the Senate 45-51 in April 2009 with the Obama administration's documented opposition. Senate Majority Whip Dick Durbin, who had offered the amendment, said publicly that the banks "frankly own the place."
- ** post-presidency analysis** (David Dayen, *The Intercept*, 28 December 2015): the program was "designed and implemented entirely by the White House" and "prioritized the health of financial institutions over homeowners."

### Sources
- Neil Barofsky, *Bailout: An Inside Account of How Washington Abandoned Main Street While Rescuing Wall Street* (Free Press, 2012).
- [David Dayen, "Obama Program That Hurt Homeowners and Helped Big Banks Is Ending," *The Intercept*, 28 December 2015](https://theintercept.com/2015/12/28/obama-program-hurt-homeowners-and-helped-big-banks-now-its-dead/)
- [ProPublica, "Secret Documents Show Weak Oversight of Key Foreclosure Program," 12 November 2012](https://www.propublica.org/article/secret-documents-show-weak-oversight-of-key-foreclosure-program)
- [PBS NewsHour, "Obama's Foreclosure Program Slammed Anew for Ineffectiveness," 3 March 2011](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/economy/obamas-foreclosure-prevention-program-has-bullet-on-its-back)
- [BillMoyers.com, "Obama's Foreclosure Relief Program Was Designed to Help Bankers, Not Homeowners," 14 February 2015](https://billmoyers.com/2015/02/14/needless-default/)
- [HuffPost, "Timothy Geithner's Treasury Department Ignored Warnings Of Mortgage Fraud: Book," 20 July 2012](https://www.huffingtonpost.com/2012/07/20/timothy-geithner-neil-barofsky_n_1686693.html)
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

### The Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
- **Signed:** 21 July 2010.
- **Content:** the principal post-2008 financial regulation legislation. Created the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (Elizabeth Warren's signature contribution from her Congressional Oversight Panel work), the Volcker Rule (restricting proprietary trading by deposit-taking banks), the Office of Financial Research, and the Financial Stability Oversight Council.
- ** post-passage analysis** (Yves Smith / *Naked Capitalism*; Matt Stoller; David Dayen): the Dodd-Frank Act left the post-2008 banking apparatus structurally intact. The post-2008 financial-policy academic position is that Dodd-Frank, documented, did not break up the too-big-to-fail banks (the largest American banks were larger in 2017 than they had been in 2008), did not restore Glass-Steagall, did not criminally prosecute the executives whose institutions had collapsed, did not impose the kind of structural reform that the contemporaneous left-Democratic faction (Sanders, Warren, the *Naked Capitalism* analytical group) had advocated. The beneficiaries of the post-2008 settlement were the creditor class. The losers were the class of foreclosed-upon households and the class of post-2008 unemployed workers.
- **The "skinny" Volcker Rule:** the implementing regulation of the Volcker Rule, published in December 2013 after extensive banking-industry lobbying, was widely characterised by the financial-policy academic literature as weakened from the original legislative intent. The Trump administration's 2018-2020 Volcker Rule rollbacks completed the bank-lobby reversal of the post-2008 reform.

### Sources
- Wikipedia, "Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act."
- post-2010 financial-reform academic literature (Stoller; Dayen; Smith).
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

## The drone war and the kill list

### Background
The pre-Obama American drone-strike record under George W. Bush totalled 57 strikes across the period 2004-2008, almost entirely in the tribal areas of Pakistan in the closing months of the Bush administration. The drone-strike program was a covert-operations program of the Central Intelligence Agency.

### Obama's expansion
- **23 January 2009** (three days into the Obama presidency): Obama authorised his first military action — two drone strikes, three hours apart, in Waziristan, Pakistan, that killed up to twenty civilians.
- **2009 full-year total:** 54 drone strikes in Pakistan alone — more strikes in Obama's first year than Bush had ordered across his entire eight-year presidency.
- **Peak year (2010):** 128 CIA drone strikes in Pakistan; at least 89 civilians killed in Pakistan that year per the Bureau of Investigative Journalism figures.
- **Obama two-term total:** 540 drone strikes across Pakistan, Yemen, and Somalia per the Council on Foreign Relations final tally (with the Bureau of Investigative Journalism count at 563). Bush total: 57 strikes. The Obama-era expansion was 10x the Bush total.
- **Civilian casualty estimates:** Bureau of Investigative Journalism recorded between 384 and 807 civilian deaths in those three countries across the Obama presidency. The independent-academic estimates from the New America Foundation recorded between 276 and 368 civilian deaths in Pakistan and Yemen across the period 2004-2013. The Obama administration figures, released in July 2016, claimed only 64-116 "non-combatants" killed in 473 counter-terrorism strikes between January 2009 and end of 2015 — figures the independent-monitoring class disputed as understating the documented civilian deaths.
- **The "guilt by association" counting method:** the administration counting method, on the 2012 documented disclosure by administration officials to the *New York Times*, counted all military-age males in a strike zone as militants unless intelligence proved otherwise — the method that produced the gap between Obama administration figures and the independent-monitoring figures.

### The "kill list" — the New York Times disclosure of 29 May 2012
- **The 29 May 2012 *New York Times* investigation** by Jo Becker and Scott Shane, "Secret 'Kill List' Proves a Test of Obama's Principles and Will," based on interviews with current and former Obama administration officials, established that:
- Obama personally oversaw the "kill list" of al-Qaeda suspects targeted for death by unmanned drone aircraft.
- Obama personally approved every drone strike in Yemen and Somalia and the "risky" strikes in Pakistan.
- The kill-list process was managed through "Terror Tuesdays" — the weekly White House meetings at which the list was reviewed and approved.
- Obama personally decided whether to launch strikes when the targets were with their families.
- The kill list included American citizens — including the 30 September 2011 drone strike in Yemen that killed Anwar al-Awlaki, a US-born American citizen, and the 14 October 2011 follow-up strike in Yemen that killed his 16-year-old American-born son Abdulrahman al-Awlaki.
- The 14 October 2011 strike on Abdulrahman al-Awlaki was, documented, never formally explained by the Obama administration; the public response from Robert Gibbs, the former White House Press Secretary, when asked about the death of the 16-year-old American citizen, was that Abdulrahman "should have had a more responsible father."
- ** White House Chief of Staff William Daley** in the *Times* report described Obama's decision to assassinate Anwar al-Awlaki, a US citizen, as "an easy one."

### Sources
- [Jo Becker and Scott Shane, "Secret 'Kill List' Proves a Test of Obama's Principles and Will," *New York Times*, 29 May 2012](https://www.nytimes.com/2012/05/29/world/obamas-leadership-in-war-on-al-qaeda.html)
- [Bureau of Investigative Journalism, "Obama's covert drone war in numbers: ten times more strikes than Bush," 17 January 2017](https://www.thebureauinvestigates.com/stories/2017-01-17/obamas-covert-drone-war-in-numbers-ten-times-more-strikes-than-bush)
- [Council on Foreign Relations, "Obama's Final Drone Strike Data," 20 January 2017](https://www.cfr.org/blog/obamas-final-drone-strike-data)
- [Democracy Now!, 29 May 2012 headlines coverage of the *Times* report](https://www.democracynow.org/2012/5/29/headlines)
- [PBS NewsHour, "How Obama Maintains His Secret 'Kill List'," 30 May 2012](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/show/how-obama-maintains-his-secret-kill-list)
- **Verified:** 18 May 2026.

## Foreign policy

### Libya 2011
- **19 March 2011:** US, UK, and French air strikes initiate the NATO intervention in the Libyan civil war under UN Security Council Resolution 1973's "responsibility to protect" mandate.
- **20 October 2011:** Muammar Gaddafi was killed by Libyan opposition forces after his convoy was disabled by a US drone strike and a French air strike near Sirte. Hillary Clinton's documented public statement on the killing — laughing on the CBS News interview that aired 20 October 2011 — was: "We came, we saw, he died."
- **Aftermath:** Libya descended into a multi-faction civil war that has continued through the period of this volume's publication. The post-Gaddafi Libyan economy — the North African oil-producer with the highest pre-2011 per-capita income on the African continent — collapsed. The subsequent migration crisis (the post-2014 Mediterranean migration of African and Middle Eastern migrants via Libyan ports) has been a driver of the post-2015 European political-class realignment that this volume's later chapters document.
- **Obama's later acknowledgment:** in the April 2016 interview with Chris Wallace on *Fox News Sunday*, Obama identified the failure to plan for post-Gaddafi Libya as "the worst mistake" of his presidency. The acknowledgment, documented, did not lead to any institutional accountability for the figures involved (Clinton at State; Samantha Power at the National Security Council; Susan Rice as UN Ambassador). The figures all continued in roles in the second Obama term.

### Syria 2011-2017
- **15 March 2011:** the Syrian uprising began as part of the Arab Spring sequence.
- **August 2011:** Obama publicly demands the Assad regime "step aside."
- **20 August 2012:** Obama publicly announces the "red line" on chemical weapons: "We have been very clear to the Assad regime, but also to other players on the ground, that a red line for us is we start seeing a whole bunch of chemical weapons moving around or being utilized."
- **21 August 2013:** Syrian government forces conducted the Ghouta chemical attack with sarin nerve gas, killing between 281 and 1,729 people in the eastern Damascus suburbs (per varying estimates from the Violations Documentation Center in Syria, Médecins Sans Frontières, and the US intelligence community).
- **31 August 2013:** Obama announces he will seek congressional authorisation for military strikes against the Assad regime. The congressional authorisation effort collapsed within days as the Republican-controlled House of Representatives indicated it would not approve the authorisation. Obama then accepted a Russian-Syrian proposal for the surrender of the declared Syrian chemical-weapons stockpile to the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons.
- **The strategic outcome:** the Obama administration's covert CIA programme of arming Syrian opposition groups (the Timber Sycamore programme, documented beginning in 2013) continued through the 2017 transition, supplying the anti-Assad opposition with weapons that the record now establishes flowed in significant quantities to jihadist factions including the al-Nusra Front and the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL). The result of the Obama Syria policy was the 2013-2014 ISIL surge, the June 2014 ISIL capture of Mosul, and the subsequent American re-engagement in Iraq.

### The Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (the Iran nuclear deal)
- **Signed:** 14 July 2015 between Iran and the P5+1 (United States, United Kingdom, France, Russia, China, plus Germany).
- **Content:** the agreement under which Iran agreed to limit its uranium enrichment to 3.67% (the civilian-power-generation level), reduce its stockpile of enriched uranium by 98%, and submit to International Atomic Energy Agency inspection in exchange for the lifting of international sanctions.
- **Outcome:** the JCPOA was the diplomatic-political success of the second Obama term. The Trump administration's May 2018 withdrawal from the JCPOA — covered in Dossier 2 — terminated the arrangement. The post-2018 Iranian nuclear programmeme has since exceeded the JCPOA enrichment limits.

### The post-2014 Russia-Ukraine policy
The Obama administration's post-2014 Russia-Ukraine policy following the Maidan revolution and the Russian annexation of Crimea is documented in Dossier 1 (Biden) above, as the diplomatic operations were run from the Vice Presidential office through the Biden portfolio.

### Cuba 2014-2016
- **17 December 2014:** Obama announced the re-establishment of diplomatic relations with Cuba after the 54-year diplomatic breach that had followed the 1961 Bay of Pigs invasion.
- **20 July 2015:** the US Embassy in Havana reopened.
- **20-22 March 2016:** Obama visited Havana — the first sitting US president to visit Cuba since Calvin Coolidge in 1928.

### The Trans-Pacific Partnership
- **Signed:** 4 February 2016.
- **Content:** the twelve-nation Pacific-Rim trade agreement that would have established the multilateral trade-and-investment apparatus binding the US, Japan, Australia, Canada, Mexico, Vietnam, Malaysia, Singapore, Brunei, Peru, Chile, and New Zealand. The TPP excluded China.
- **Outcome:** the 2016 American presidential campaign produced opposition from both Trump (Republican) and Sanders (Democratic primary) and Clinton (Democratic nominee, after primary pressure from Sanders) to the TPP. The Obama administration did not submit the TPP to Congress for ratification before the November 2016 election. The Trump administration withdrew the US from the TPP on 23 January 2017.

## Domestic policy

### Healthcare — the Affordable Care Act
- **Signed:** 23 March 2010.
- **Content:** the healthcare-reform legislation that expanded Medicaid eligibility, established the state-and-federal health-insurance exchanges, mandated the individual purchase of health insurance (the "individual mandate"), and prohibited insurer practices including the exclusion of pre-existing conditions. The public-option provision that the left-Democratic faction had pushed for was removed from the final bill in the negotiation with the centrist Democratic senators (Joe Lieberman, Ben Nelson). The single-payer proposal was never seriously considered.
- **Outcome:** the ACA reduced the American uninsured rate from 18% in 2010 to 8% by 2016. The ACA structure preserved the dominance of the American private health-insurance industry; the post-ACA American per-capita healthcare expenditure remained the highest in the OECD by a wide margin. The ACA was the signature domestic-legislative achievement of the Obama presidency.

### Immigration enforcement
- **The deportation record:** the Obama administration deported 3 million unauthorised immigrants across the two terms — more deportations than any prior American administration in absolute numerical terms. The immigrant-rights advocacy class labelled Obama the "Deporter-in-Chief" across the 2014-2016 period. The 2014 surge in deportations of Central American migrants (the 2014 "border crisis") was managed through the expansion of the immigration-detention facilities that the Trump first-term immigration policy would subsequently inherit and expand.
- **DACA (Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals):** announced 15 June 2012 by the executive memorandum from Secretary of Homeland Security Janet Napolitano. The program protected 800,000 unauthorised immigrants who had been brought to the United States as children from deportation. The program was the administrative-action response to the failed DREAM Act legislative effort.

### Mass surveillance and the Edward Snowden disclosures
- **June 2013:** the Edward Snowden disclosures, published by the *Guardian*, the *Washington Post*, and other outlets, established that the National Security Agency (NSA) was conducting mass surveillance of American citizens through the PRISM programme and the bulk-telephone-metadata-collection programme under Section 215 of the USA PATRIOT Act. The Obama administration's response to the Snowden disclosures was to charge Snowden under the Espionage Act, to defend the surveillance programmes as necessary national-security measures, and to reform the bulk-metadata-collection programme through the 2015 USA FREEDOM Act after the Second Circuit Court of Appeals had ruled in May 2015 that the bulk-collection programme was unlawful.
- **The Espionage Act prosecutions:** the Obama administration charged whistleblowers under the 1917 Espionage Act more often than all prior American administrations combined — Snowden, Chelsea Manning, John Kiriakou, Thomas Drake, Stephen Jin-Woo Kim, Jeffrey Sterling, Donald Sachtleben, James Hitselberger. The pattern was the subject of the contemporaneous Committee to Protect Journalists analysis (October 2013) that the Obama administration was the worst administration for press freedom since the Nixon administration.

## Post-presidency

### Commercial arrangements
- **February 2017:** Joint $65 million book deal with Penguin Random House for the Barack Obama and Michelle Obama memoirs. Michelle Obama's *Becoming* was published in November 2018 and became the best-selling memoir in American publishing history within twelve months. Barack Obama's *A Promised Land* was published in November 2020.
- **22 May 2018:** Higher Ground Productions multi-year deal with Netflix announced. The financial terms were not publicly disclosed; the industry estimates placed the deal value at $50 million across the initial term, with the comparable Netflix talent deals (Shonda Rhimes' $150 million 5-year deal; Ryan Murphy's $300 million 5-year deal) providing the comparable benchmarks.
- **Speaking fees:** Obama's post-presidency speaking-engagement fees were documented at $400,000 per appearance. The 2017 disclosure of a $400,000 speaking fee paid by Wall Street firm Cantor Fitzgerald to Obama for a September 2017 speech was the post-presidency commercial transaction that drew public attention to the post-presidency Wall Street monetisation. The 2017 Senator Elizabeth Warren public comment on the speaking fee: "I was troubled by that. One of the things I talk about in the book is the influence of money. I describe it as a snake that slithers through Washington."
- **Property acquisitions:** the Obamas purchased a $11.75 million estate at 79 Turkeyland Cove Road, Edgartown, Martha's Vineyard, in August 2019. The Washington D.C. property at 2446 Belmont Road NW, Kalorama, was acquired in May 2017 for $8.1 million. The Honolulu acquisition (3725 Beach Road, Waimanalo) was acquired in 2021 for an undisclosed amount believed to be in the $9 million range.

### The Obama Foundation
- **Founded:** 2014.
- **Mission:** the post-presidency civic-engagement-and-leadership foundation, with the Obama Presidential Center planned for the Jackson Park location on the south side of Chicago. The centre construction has been the subject of ongoing community-and-historic-preservation disputes through the 2017-2025 period; the original 2017 projected completion date of 2021 has been revised multiple times.

### Sources
- [NPR, "Obamas Sign Deal With Netflix, Form 'Higher Ground Productions'," 22 May 2018](https://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2018/05/22/613246456/obamas-sign-content-deal-with-netlfix-form-higher-ground-productions)
- [*Hollywood Reporter*, "Obamas Ink Producing Deal With Netflix," 22 May 2018](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/obamas-ink-producing-deal-netflix-1113672/)
- [*Spokesman-Review*, "The Obamas sign a jumbo book deal with Penguin Random House," 26 December 2020](https://www.spokesman.com/stories/2020/dec/26/the-obamas-sign-a-jumbo-book-deal-with-penguin-ran/)

# Sources — Dossier 4: Hillary Diane Rodham Clinton

## Biographical
- Born 26 October 1947, Chicago, Illinois.
- Father Hugh Ellsworth Rodham (1911-1993), textile-business owner; mother Dorothy Emma Howell Rodham (1919-2011), homemaker.
- Maine South High School graduate 1965.
- Wellesley College 1965-1969 (BA in political science); student-government president and commencement speaker 1969.
- Yale Law School 1969-1973 (JD).
- 1974: staff attorney on the House Judiciary Committee's impeachment inquiry into Richard Nixon (Watergate).
- Married Bill Clinton 11 October 1975 in Fayetteville, Arkansas.
- Partner at the Rose Law Firm in Little Rock, Arkansas 1979.
- First Lady of Arkansas 1979-1981 and 1983-1992 (Bill Clinton's gubernatorial tenures).
- First Lady of the United States 20 January 1993 - 20 January 2001.
- United States Senator from New York 3 January 2001 - 21 January 2009.
- 67th US Secretary of State 21 January 2009 - 1 February 2013.
- Democratic Party presidential nominee 2016.

## The Rose Law Firm and Whitewater period (1979-1992)
- The commercial-political controversy of the pre-Presidency period was the Whitewater Development Corporation investment. The Clintons invested in the Whitewater real-estate venture in 1978 with James and Susan McDougal; the venture was associated with the Madison Guaranty Savings & Loan, the savings-and-loan institution operated by James McDougal that subsequently failed in the savings-and-loan crisis of the late 1980s and cost American taxpayers $60 million in deposit-insurance liability.
- The Rose Law Firm represented Madison Guaranty across the period of Hillary Clinton's partnership at the firm. The billing-records controversy across the 1993-1998 Whitewater investigation period produced the subpoena of Rose Law Firm billing records that the Clinton White House initially could not locate; the billing records subsequently appeared in the White House residence book room in January 1996, two years after they had been subpoenaed.
- The Independent Counsel investigation under Robert Fiske (1994) and Kenneth Starr (1994-2002) produced no indictment of Bill or Hillary Clinton, though the investigation produced fifteen convictions including those of James and Susan McDougal (Whitewater) and Webster Hubbell (Hillary Clinton's former Rose Law Firm partner who served at the Justice Department under Bill Clinton).

## First Ladyship 1993-2001
### The Task Force on National Health Care Reform
- **Established:** 25 January 1993, five days into the Bill Clinton presidency.
- **Chair:** Hillary Clinton.
- **Outcome:** the Task Force health-reform legislation (the "Clinton health plan") was defeated in the 103rd Congress in September 1994 without a floor vote. The defeat was attributed in the contemporary political-class analysis to the insurance-industry "Harry and Louise" advertising campaign, the procedural mishandling by the Task Force (which operated in closed sessions across 1993, producing the "Hillarycare" public-relations characterisation), and the centrist-Democratic-and-Republican opposition.
- ** consequence:** the Democratic-Party 1994 midterm-election loss of both chambers of Congress to the Republican Party — the first Republican congressional majority since 1954. The 1994 Republican wave was directly attributed in the contemporary post-election analysis to the failure of the health-care reform programme.

### Travelgate
- **May 1993:** the White House Travel Office firings. Seven White House Travel Office employees were dismissed; the firings produced allegations that Hillary Clinton had pressed for the firings in order to redirect the travel-services business to a Clinton-friendly travel agency (World Wide Travel of Little Rock).
- ** 1996 House investigation finding:** the House Government Reform and Oversight Committee Republican investigation concluded that Hillary Clinton had played a role in the firings, contradicting the Clinton public statements at the time.
- ** 2000 Independent Counsel Robert Ray report:** Hillary Clinton had given "factually false" statements about her involvement, but the false statements did not rise to the criminal-prosecution threshold.

### Vince Foster
- **20 July 1993:** Vince Foster, the Deputy White House Counsel and former Rose Law Firm partner of Hillary Clinton, was found dead at Fort Marcy Park, Virginia, of a gunshot wound to the head. The official conclusion across five separate investigations (the US Park Police, the Department of Justice, the Special Counsel Robert Fiske, the Independent Counsel Kenneth Starr, and the Senate Whitewater Committee) was that the death was suicide. The conspiracy theories surrounding the Foster death have continued to circulate throughout the post-1993 American political-class commentary; the official investigations have remained the official record.

## Senate 2001-2009
### The 2002 Iraq AUMF vote
- **11 October 2002:** Senator Clinton voted for the Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution, the congressional authorisation for the 2003 invasion of Iraq.
- ** 2007-2008 primary campaign:** Clinton's 2002 Iraq vote was the issue exploited by the Obama primary campaign, which contrasted Obama's then-state-senator 2002 anti-war public statements with Clinton's 2002 yes vote. The contrast was the decisive issue of the Iowa caucus victory by Obama in January 2008 and the subsequent Obama primary momentum.
- ** 2014 apology:** Clinton publicly described her 2002 vote as a "mistake" in her 2014 memoir *Hard Choices*. The apology came twelve years after the vote, six years after the Iraq vote had cost her the 2008 Democratic nomination.

### Senate banking record
- Clinton served on the Senate Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs Committee 2001-2002. She received $733,000 in 2007-2008 cycle contributions from the securities-and-investment sector — among the largest 2008 primary cycle Wall Street fundraising totals.

## Secretary of State 2009-2013

### The pre-Confirmation ethics arrangement
- **December 2008:** Hillary Clinton, before her confirmation as Secretary of State, signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Obama administration regarding the Clinton Foundation's foreign-donor relationships. The MOU prohibited new foreign-government donations to the Foundation during Clinton's Secretary of State tenure unless the State Department ethics officials approved them; the MOU permitted continuing donations from foreign governments that had donated to the Foundation prior to the MOU.
- ** Senate Foreign Relations Committee statement** (Senator Richard Lugar, the Republican on the committee, January 2009 confirmation hearing): the Clinton Foundation was "a unique complication that would have to be managed with great care and transparency"; the Foundation could create "the impression that foreign donors were giving money to gain access at the State Department."

### foreign-government donations during the Secretary tenure
- The Clinton Foundation accepted donations from at least seven foreign governments during Clinton's Secretary tenure (2009-2013) in continuation of pre-existing donor relationships permitted under the MOU: Australia, the Dominican Republic, Kuwait, Norway, Qatar, Oman, the United Kingdom.
- **The Algerian donation:** the Clinton Foundation accepted a $500,000 donation from Algeria for the Haiti relief fund in 2010 without the State Department ethics review the MOU required. The Washington Post 25 February 2015 reporting documented that the Algerian donation "coincided with a spike" in Algerian government lobbying of the State Department on the Algerian human-rights record.
- **The Clinton Health Access Initiative (CHAI) failure:** the CHAI subsidiary of the Clinton Foundation failed to report donors from 2009 to 2013, a violation of the MOU disclosure requirements. The CHAI spokesperson attributed the omission to "oversight."

### The UBS settlement
- **2009:** the IRS investigation of UBS, the Swiss bank, over the offshore-tax-shelter accounts of 52,000 Americans was the tax-enforcement priority of the Obama Justice Department and Treasury. The settlement, brokered by Clinton in her Secretary of State capacity in February 2009, produced UBS's agreement to provide the identities of 4,450 American account holders out of the 52,000 the IRS had requested.
- ** post-settlement donations:** UBS's cumulative donations to the Clinton Foundation grew from less than $60,000 through 2008 to $600,000 by end of 2014. UBS additionally paid Bill Clinton $1.5 million for a series of question-and-answer sessions with UBS Wealth Management CEO Bob McCann across 2011-2014 — the largest single corporate-speech-income source for Bill Clinton in the post-Presidency period.
- ** Wall Street Journal documentation** (April 2015 Grimaldi and Ballhaus reporting): the UBS sequence — settlement, donations increase, $1.5M Bill Clinton speaking fees — was the contemporaneous documented commercial-political arrangement between the Secretary of State's family foundation and the multinational bank whose IRS investigation the Secretary had brokered the settlement of.

### Libya 2011
- The Libya intervention is covered in Dossier 3 (Obama) above. Clinton was the diplomatic principal of the March 2011 NATO intervention; her post-Gaddafi 20 October 2011 CBS News public statement — "We came, we saw, he died" — is documented. Obama's April 2016 acknowledgment that the failure to plan for post-Gaddafi Libya was "the worst mistake" of his presidency is documented.

### Benghazi (11 September 2012)
- **11 September 2012:** the coordinated armed attack on the US diplomatic compound and the CIA annex at Benghazi, Libya, killed US Ambassador to Libya J. Christopher Stevens, US Foreign Service Information Management Officer Sean Smith, and CIA contractors Tyrone S. Woods and Glen Doherty.
- ** 2012-2016 post-Benghazi investigations:** the Accountability Review Board (the State Department internal review, December 2012); the six congressional investigations including the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence and the House Select Committee on Benghazi (2014-2016 under Chairman Trey Gowdy). The cumulative investigations produced criticism of the State Department's pre-attack security posture but did not produce findings of criminal misconduct by Hillary Clinton.
- ** 22 October 2015 Hillary Clinton testimony:** the eleven-hour public testimony before the House Select Committee on Benghazi was the public-record moment at which the Clinton 2016 presidential campaign survived the Benghazi political-class controversy that had been built into the 2014-2015 House investigation.

### The private email-server controversy
- **January 2009:** Hillary Clinton established a private email server (clintonemail.com) operated from her Chappaqua, New York residence for her State Department official correspondence. The arrangement bypassed the State.gov system that the State Department's records-retention policy required for official correspondence.
- **March 2015:** the public disclosure of the private server arrangement by the *New York Times*.
- ** FBI investigation 2015-2016:** the FBI investigation under Director James Comey examined 30,000 emails from the server. findings: at least 110 emails contained classified information at the time they were sent; eight email chains contained Top Secret information.
- ** 5 July 2016 FBI Director Comey statement:** Clinton and her aides had been "extremely careless" in their handling of classified information, but the conduct did not rise to the criminal-prosecution threshold; the FBI recommended against indictment.
- ** 28 October 2016 Comey letter:** Comey publicly announced eleven days before the 8 November 2016 election that the FBI was reopening the email investigation based on new emails discovered on Anthony Weiner's laptop (Weiner's wife, Huma Abedin, was Clinton's aide). The 6 November 2016 Comey follow-up letter, two days before the election, announced that the new emails had been reviewed and did not change the July recommendation.
- ** Clinton post-election analysis:** Clinton in her 2017 memoir *What Happened* identified the 28 October Comey letter as the decisive event of the 2016 election. The post-2016 political-class analysis has continued to dispute the question of whether the Comey letter or the structural Wall Street-and-Foundation donor-pattern issues were the decisive 2016 factor.

### Uranium One
- **2009-2013:** Uranium One, a Canadian uranium-mining company with US mining stakes including 20% of US uranium-production capacity, was acquired in stages by Rosatom, the Russian state atomic-energy corporation. The acquisition required approval from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS), which included the State Department.
- ** Clinton Foundation donations:** during the 2008-2013 period of the Uranium One acquisition negotiations, individuals associated with the Uranium One transaction (including Uranium One Chairman Ian Telfer and figures from the Frank Giustra Mining Group) donated amounts totalling $145 million to the Clinton Foundation. The donations were not publicly disclosed at the time of the CFIUS approval despite the Clinton-Foundation public-disclosure obligations.
- ** 23 April 2015 New York Times investigation** by Jo Becker and Mike McIntire, "Cash Flowed to Clinton Foundation Amid Russian Uranium Deal," established the record of the parallel commercial-and-political arrangements.
- ** post-2017 Trump administration investigation:** the 2017-2018 Trump Department of Justice review of the Uranium One transaction did not produce criminal charges. The Republican-investigative position was that the absence of charges reflected post-2017 institutional protection rather than the absence of factual misconduct. The Democratic-investigative position was that the CFIUS approval had been a nine-agency decision in which the State Department was one vote among nine; the individual Clinton role in the decision was, on the evidence, the subject of contested interpretation.

## The 2016 presidential campaign

### The 2016 Democratic primary and the DNC tilt
- ** Democratic primary 12 February - 14 June 2016:** Hillary Clinton against Senator Bernie Sanders. Clinton won 16.9 million primary votes to Sanders's 13.2 million; Clinton won the pledged-delegate count 2,205 to 1,846; Clinton's superdelegate lead at the commencement of the primary cycle was 458 to 7. The Democratic primary was the contest in which the Clinton 2008 institutional Democratic-party support apparatus was the 2016 Clinton support apparatus from the beginning.
- ** 22 July 2016 WikiLeaks DNC email release:** 20,000 DNC emails dating from January 2015 through May 2016, leaked to WikiLeaks and published on 22 July 2016 immediately before the Democratic National Convention. The emails included DNC officials discussing the Sanders primary campaign with contempt and proposing ways to undermine Sanders' candidacy (including the proposal to raise questions about Sanders' Jewish faith in Southern primary states). The content of the emails confirmed the Sanders-campaign-and-Sanders-supporter pre-leak allegations that the DNC had not maintained the procedural neutrality required of the DNC under the DNC charter.
- **24 July 2016:** DNC Chair Debbie Wasserman Schultz announced her resignation effective at the end of the Democratic Convention. The resignation was the public acknowledgment of the procedural impropriety the emails had documented. Donna Brazile, the DNC Vice-Chair, became the interim DNC Chair.
- ** post-2016 Donna Brazile disclosures:** in her 2017 memoir *Hacks: The Inside Story of the Break-ins and Breakdowns That Put Donald Trump in the White House*, Brazile documented the August 2015 Joint Fundraising Agreement between the Clinton campaign, the DNC, and the Hillary Victory Fund — an agreement that, on Brazile's documentary record, gave the Clinton campaign control over the DNC operations and staff hiring across the 2015-2016 primary cycle. The Brazile conclusion: "the funding arrangement... was not illegal, but it sure looked unethical."

### The Wall Street speaking-fee record (2013-2015)
- ** post-State-Department speaking-fee period January 2014 - March 2015:** 53 paid speeches at a standard fee of $225,000, totalling $11.8 million in fees in the 14-month period.
- ** 2013 speaking-fee period (pre-disclosure):** the three Goldman Sachs speeches at $225,000 each, totalling $675,000. The speeches were delivered before the 2015 disclosure period and were not on the public-record disclosure forms until the 2015 Sanders-campaign pressure forced the disclosure.
- ** 2013-2015 total cycle:** 92 paid speeches totalling $21.6 million per the CNN Money analysis. 8 speeches to major banks netted $1.8 million per the same analysis.
- ** Sanders-campaign pressure for transcript release:** Clinton publicly committed during the 2016 primary to release the Goldman Sachs transcripts only if all other 2016 candidates released their speech transcripts. The commitment was the 2016 primary-period dodge that allowed Clinton to avoid disclosing the content of the Goldman Sachs speeches.
- ** 7 October 2016 *The Intercept* publication:** the WikiLeaks release of Clinton campaign chair John Podesta's emails included excerpts from the Goldman Sachs and other speaking-fee transcripts. The excerpts documented Clinton's statements at the 2013 paid events, including the public-vs-private-position statement to a 2013 housing trade group: "If everybody's watching, you know, all of the back room discussions and the deals, you know, then people get a little nervous, to say the least. So, you need both a public and a private position." The statement was the documented Clinton acknowledgment of the dual-position political-class operational mode.
- ** Simpson-Bowles endorsement:** at a April 2013 Morgan Stanley speech, Clinton praised the Simpson-Bowles deficit reduction plan that included corporate-tax-rate reductions and Social Security retirement-age increases. The endorsement contradicted her 2016 public campaign positions against the Simpson-Bowles proposals.

### The 8 November 2016 election
- ** result:** Donald Trump 304 Electoral College votes, Hillary Clinton 227. Clinton won the national popular vote by 2.9 million votes (65,853,514 to 62,984,828). Trump won the Electoral College through narrow victories in Pennsylvania (44,292 vote margin), Wisconsin (22,748 vote margin), and Michigan (10,704 vote margin) — a combined 77,744-vote margin in three Midwestern states that the Clinton campaign had not visited in the closing weeks of the campaign in the assumption that the Clinton "blue wall" would hold.
- ** post-election Clinton analysis** (her 2017 memoir *What Happened*): Clinton identified the 28 October Comey letter, the Russian interference (the 2017 US Intelligence Community Assessment of Russian interference in the 2016 election), the misogyny of the American electorate, and the structural deficiencies of the Democratic Party as the 2016 election factors.
- ** post-2016 left-Democratic analysis** (Thomas Frank, *Listen, Liberal*; Matt Stoller; the Sanders-aligned analytical class): the Clinton campaign had failed to make the economic-populist case to the post-2008 Rust Belt working-class American voter; the campaign had been built on the identity-politics-and-celebrity-endorsement apparatus; the Wall Street speaking-fee record had been the structural credibility problem the campaign had been unable to overcome.

## Post-2016
- **2017:** *What Happened* memoir published September 2017.
- **2017-2020:** Onward Together political-action group founded; the post-2016 Clinton political-class operations continued through the political-action and the Democratic National Convention 2020 virtual-keynote appearances.
- **2020:** Clinton served as the Democratic-elector representative of New York at the December 2020 Electoral College certification of Biden's election.
- **2023-present:** the Hillary Rodham Clinton Presidential Fellows programme at Columbia University; the 2024-2025 public-commentary on the post-2024 American political-class period.

## Cross-references
- Volume One Chapter 7: post-1971 American political-class consolidation; the Clintons are the post-1968 Democratic-party operators of the Reagan-Bush settlement's centrist-Democratic continuation.
- Dossier 3 (Obama): the Clinton-Obama 2008 primary contest and the subsequent Clinton Secretary of State tenure.
- Dossier 2 (Trump): the 2016 Clinton-Trump general-election contest that produced the Trump first presidency.
- Future Dossier on Bill Clinton: the Bill Clinton commercial-and-political portfolio across the 1993-2025 period that Hillary Clinton's portfolio operated alongside.
- Future Dossier on Joe Biden (already published as Dossier 1): the post-2009 Democratic-Party-administrative continuity.
