# Sources — Dossier 5: William Jefferson Clinton

## Biographical
- Born 19 August 1946, Hope, Arkansas, as William Jefferson Blythe III. Father William Jefferson Blythe Jr. (1918-1946) died in an automobile accident three months before Bill's birth. Mother Virginia Cassidy Blythe (1923-1994) married Roger Clinton Sr. in 1950; Bill formally adopted the Clinton surname in 1962.
- Hot Springs High School graduate 1964.
- Georgetown University School of Foreign Service BA 1968.
- Rhodes Scholar at University College, Oxford 1968-1970.
- Yale Law School JD 1973.
- Married Hillary Rodham 11 October 1975 in Fayetteville, Arkansas.
- Arkansas Attorney General 1977-1979.
- Governor of Arkansas 1979-1981 and 1983-1992 (lost re-election in 1980 to Republican Frank White; regained the office in 1982).
- 42nd President of the United States 20 January 1993 - 20 January 2001.
- Post-presidency: founded the William J. Clinton Foundation in 2001 (renamed the Bill, Hillary & Chelsea Clinton Foundation in 2013).

## The 1992 election
- Won the Democratic nomination through the Super Tuesday Southern primaries victory after the New Hampshire "Comeback Kid" second-place finish.
- Won the general election with 43.0% of the popular vote (370 Electoral College votes) against George H.W. Bush (37.5%, 168 EC) and Ross Perot (18.9%, 0 EC).
- The Perot third-party candidacy is documented as a significant factor in the 1992 outcome; the subsequent post-1992 analysis has contested the question of the counterfactual without Perot.

## The 1993-2001 presidency

### NAFTA — the North American Free Trade Agreement
- **Signed:** 8 December 1993 by Clinton.
- **Effective:** 1 January 1994.
- **Negotiated:** under the George H.W. Bush administration in 1992; Clinton post-election position was to ratify with the labour-and-environmental side agreements that the 1993 congressional negotiations produced.
- ** outcome dispute:**
- US Chamber of Commerce: NAFTA increased US trade in goods and services with Canada and Mexico from $337 billion in 1993 to $1.2 trillion in 2011.
- AFL-CIO: NAFTA caused 700,000 American manufacturing jobs to shift to Mexico over the same period.
- The post-2000 academic-economic-analysis literature (Robert Scott of the Economic Policy Institute; Ian Fletcher; the post-2010 industrial-policy revival faction) has documented the NAFTA-and-WTO accession-driven 1995-2010 American manufacturing-employment collapse as a structural cause of the post-2008 Rust Belt political-class realignment that produced the 2016 Trump campaign.

### The 1994 Crime Bill
- **Signed:** 13 September 1994.
- **Formal name:** Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994.
- **Content:** $30.2 billion appropriation across the 1994-2000 period, including funding for 100,000 new police officers, the federal three-strikes provision (mandatory life imprisonment for third felony convictions), the federal death-penalty expansion (60 new federal capital offences), the Federal Assault Weapons Ban (1994-2004), and the Violence Against Women Act.
- ** contemporary co-sponsor:** Senator Joe Biden (D-Delaware) — covered in Dossier 1.
- ** outcome:** the 1994 Crime Bill is documented in the post-2000 academic-policy analysis as a contributing factor in the 1990s-and-2000s American mass-incarceration expansion. The 2008 federal prison population was 2.3 million people, up from 1 million in 1990 — the largest carceral population in absolute and per-capita terms in the world.
- ** 2016 acknowledgment:** Hillary Clinton publicly acknowledged in her 2016 primary campaign that the 1994 Crime Bill had produced unintended mass-incarceration consequences. Bill Clinton himself, in his April 2016 confrontation with Black Lives Matter protesters at a Philadelphia campaign rally, publicly defended the bill on the record before subsequently acknowledging the unintended consequences.

### Welfare Reform — the 1996 PRWORA
- **Signed:** 22 August 1996.
- **Formal name:** Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996.
- **Content:** the replacement of Aid to Families with Dependent Children (AFDC) — the federal entitlement programme established under Franklin Roosevelt's Social Security Act of 1935 — with Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF), a block-grant programme administered by the states with five-year lifetime limits on benefits.
- ** contemporary opposition:** the 1996 Clinton administration internal opposition included Department of Health and Human Services Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation Peter Edelman, who resigned in protest. Edelman's 1997 *Atlantic Monthly* essay, "The Worst Thing Bill Clinton Has Done," documented the post-PRWORA welfare-roll-and-poverty-outcome data.
- ** outcome:** the post-1996 welfare rolls fell 60% by 2000 per the administration accounting; the post-2008 academic-poverty-research literature (Edin and Shaefer, *$2.00 a Day: Living on Almost Nothing in America*, 2015) documented the post-1996 expansion of deep poverty — American households with under-$2-per-day cash incomes — to 1.5 million households by 2011.

### The Glass-Steagall repeal — the 1999 Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act
- **Signed:** 12 November 1999.
- **Formal name:** Financial Services Modernization Act of 1999.
- **Content:** repealed the 1933 Glass-Steagall Act provisions (Sections 20 and 32) that had separated commercial banking from investment banking and insurance. The repeal permitted bank-holding companies to combine commercial banking, investment banking, and insurance operations within a single corporate structure.
- ** precursor:** the 1998 Citicorp-Travelers Group merger that created Citigroup was the pre-GLBA merger that the repeal subsequently legalised. The Citigroup merger had been permitted under the 1956 Bank Holding Company Act's five-year grace period; the 1999 GLBA legalised the already-completed merger.
- ** Treasury Secretary at the 1999 negotiation:** Robert Rubin (Treasury Secretary 1995-1999), who left Treasury for Citigroup in October 1999 and received $126 million in Citigroup compensation across the subsequent 2000-2008 period. Lawrence Summers succeeded Rubin as Treasury Secretary and signed the GLBA on Clinton's behalf.
- ** Clinton public statement on signing (12 November 1999):** "This is a day we can celebrate as an American day... the Glass-Steagall law is no longer appropriate for the economy in which we live... today what we are doing is modernizing the financial services industry, tearing down these antiquated laws and granting banks significant new authority."
- ** post-2008 academic-finance analysis:** the Joseph Stiglitz position (Nobel Memorial Prize in Economics, 2001) was that the post-GLBA "investment-bank culture came out on top" in the merged commercial-and-investment-bank holding companies, with the consequence that the risk-taking culture of investment banking displaced the conservative culture of commercial banking. The Elizabeth Warren position is documented: Glass-Steagall had kept banks from doing "crazy things" and the FDIC insurance, the Glass-Steagall separation, and the SEC regulations had together provided "50 years without a crisis" before the GLBA. The alternative position (Alan Blinder; Melanie Fein; Ben Bernanke) was that the 2008 crisis was driven by shadow-banking-firm activities that the GLBA had not authorised. The evidence-based conclusion is documentary: the GLBA was the 1999 deregulation that the major banking lobby had pursued for two decades; the 1998 Citigroup merger had pre-positioned the need for the repeal; the Treasury Secretary Rubin transitioned to Citigroup compensation immediately after the repeal; and the post-2008 academic position that GLBA was a contributing factor in the 2008 collapse is held by the critical-finance-academic class while the alternative position is held by post-2008 banking-defender academic figures.

### The Commodity Futures Modernization Act of 2000
- **Signed:** 21 December 2000 by Clinton.
- **Content:** the legislative exemption of over-the-counter derivatives — including credit-default swaps and collateralised debt obligations — from CFTC and SEC regulatory oversight.
- ** contemporaneous opposition:** Brooksley Born, the CFTC Chair from 1996 to 1999, publicly warned across her 1997-1999 tenure that the unregulated OTC-derivatives market presented systemic risk. The Clinton administration's Treasury team (Robert Rubin; Lawrence Summers; Federal Reserve Chair Alan Greenspan) publicly opposed Born's regulatory proposals. Born resigned in June 1999 after the 1998 Long-Term Capital Management collapse — the pre-2008 warning of the systemic-risk profile of unregulated derivatives — produced no regulatory response.
- ** outcome:** the unregulated OTC-derivatives market grew from $80 trillion in notional value at signing to $683 trillion at the 2008 collapse, per the Bank for International Settlements figures. The 2008 financial collapse was, on the subsequent FCIC (Financial Crisis Inquiry Commission) 2011 report findings, driven in significant part by the unregulated derivatives market that the 2000 CFMA had legalised.

### Bombing of Iraq and Sudan; Kosovo intervention
- **December 1998:** Operation Desert Fox — the US-UK air-strike campaign against Iraq for non-compliance with UN weapons-inspection requirements, ordered three days before the House impeachment vote.
- **August 1998:** Operation Infinite Reach — the US cruise-missile strikes on the al-Qaeda training camps in Afghanistan and the al-Shifa pharmaceutical plant in Khartoum, Sudan, in retaliation for the 7 August 1998 East African embassy bombings. The al-Shifa strike has been the subject of post-1998 contestation; the factory was the Sudanese pharmaceutical-production facility, and the intelligence basis for the chemical-weapons-production claim was subsequently disputed.
- **March-June 1999:** the 78-day NATO air campaign against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (Serbia) over the Kosovo conflict. The intervention was conducted without UN Security Council authorisation. The Kosovo independence declaration came in 2008.

### The Lewinsky scandal and impeachment

The 1995-1997 Bill Clinton sexual relationship with Monica Lewinsky, a 21-year-old unpaid White House intern at the commencement of the relationship, became the subject of the 1998 Independent Counsel Kenneth Starr investigation when Linda Tripp (Lewinsky's Pentagon co-worker) provided FBI agents with recordings of conversations with Lewinsky describing the relationship.

- **17 January 1998:** Clinton deposed under oath in the Paula Jones sexual-harassment civil suit; denied sexual relationship with Lewinsky.
- **26 January 1998:** Clinton's televised public statement: "I did not have sexual relations with that woman, Miss Lewinsky."
- **17 August 1998:** Clinton testified before the federal grand jury in the Starr investigation. Acknowledged "inappropriate" conduct with Lewinsky in subsequent televised public statement but did not admit to perjury, instructing others to lie, or cover-up.
- **9 September 1998:** Starr submitted the Starr Report to the House of Representatives; report alleged eleven grounds for impeachment including perjury, obstruction of justice, witness tampering, and abuse of power.
- **19 December 1998:** House voted to impeach Clinton on two articles — Article I (perjury before the grand jury) and Article III (obstruction of justice). Articles II and IV (perjury in the Paula Jones civil deposition, and abuse of power) failed in the House. Clinton became the second American President impeached, the first being Andrew Johnson in 1868.
- **12 February 1999:** Senate acquitted Clinton on both articles. Article I (perjury): 45 not guilty (45 Democrats and 10 Republicans), 55 guilty (45 Republicans). Article III (obstruction): 50 not guilty (45 Democrats and 5 Republicans), 50 guilty (50 Republicans). The 67-vote conviction threshold was not met on either article.
- **12 April 1999:** Federal District Judge Susan Webber Wright cited Clinton for civil contempt of court for his "willful failure" to obey her order to testify truthfully in the Paula Jones deposition. The $90,000 fine was assessed; the matter was referred to the Arkansas Supreme Court for disciplinary action.
- **19 January 2001:** the day before leaving office, Clinton entered an agreement with the Independent Counsel under which he admitted to false testimony, had his Arkansas law licence suspended for five years, and paid a $25,000 fine to the Arkansas Bar Association — in exchange for the Independent Counsel's agreement not to pursue post-Presidency criminal charges.

### The Marc Rich pardon
- **20 January 2001:** Clinton's last day in office. Clinton issued 140 pardons and 36 commutations, the largest single-day pardon-and-commutation total of his presidency.
- **The Marc Rich pardon:** Marc Rich, a commodities trader, had been indicted in 1983 on 65 counts including systematic tax fraud evading more than $48 million in tax liability, wire fraud, racketeering, and illegal oil-trading transactions with Iran during the 1979-1980 hostage crisis. Rich fled the United States immediately after the indictment and remained in Switzerland for the subsequent 18 years on the FBI Ten Most Wanted list. Clinton pardoned Rich on the 20 January 2001 last-day pardon list.
- ** pre-pardon donations:** Denise Eisenberg Rich, Marc Rich's former wife, had donated $450,000 to the Clinton Presidential Library Foundation, over $1 million to Democratic Party campaigns across the 1990s, and contributions to Hillary Clinton's 2000 Senate campaign across the pre-pardon period.
- ** pardon procedure:** the Rich pardon bypassed the Department of Justice Office of the Pardon Attorney, the conventional procedural route. The internal Clinton administration record (subsequent testimony by John Podesta before Congress) was that White House aides including Podesta had urged Clinton not to grant the pardon.
- ** 2001-2005 federal investigation:** the federal investigation into the pardon was opened by Manhattan US Attorney Mary Jo White in 2001, transferred to her successor James Comey (the subsequent FBI Director who would, fifteen years later, send the 28 October 2016 letter to Congress that would feature in the Hillary Clinton dossier), and closed in 2005 without charges.
- ** subsequent Clinton acknowledgment:** Clinton publicly acknowledged in his subsequent autobiographical and interview statements that the Rich pardon was "terrible politics" and "wasn't worth the damage to my reputation."

## Post-presidency (2001-2026)

### Speaking fees
- The post-Presidency Bill Clinton speaking-fee record across 2001-2015 totalled $105 million across 542 paid speeches per the 2015 *Washington Post* analysis of Hillary Clinton federal financial-disclosure forms.
- The largest single-corporate-source speaking-fee total: $1.5 million from UBS, the Swiss bank, across the 2011-2014 question-and-answer-series sessions with UBS Wealth Management CEO Bob McCann. The UBS arrangement is on the 2009 IRS-settlement record covered in Dossier 4 (Hillary Clinton).
- The 2001-2015 speaking-fee record was the post-Presidency commercial-political portfolio that built the Clinton family financial position from the "we were dead broke" 2000 public statement Hillary Clinton made about the post-White House financial position to the estimated $200-million-plus Clinton family net worth by 2015.

### The Clinton Foundation
- Founded 2001 as the William J. Clinton Foundation.
- Renamed Bill, Hillary & Chelsea Clinton Foundation 2013.
- global-public-health programmes through the Clinton Health Access Initiative (CHAI); global-development programmes through the Clinton Global Initiative (founded 2005).
- 2009-2015 foreign-donation portfolio is covered in Dossier 4 (Hillary Clinton).
- The 2015 *New York Times* "Clinton Cash" reporting by Peter Schweizer documented the pattern of commercial-political arrangements between the Clinton Foundation donors and the US State Department under Hillary Clinton's tenure.

### The Jeffrey Epstein relationship

The post-Presidency Bill Clinton relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, the American financier and convicted child-sex offender, is documented across the 2002-2003 period.

- ** flight-log record:** the FAA flight logs for Epstein's Boeing 727 jet (registration N908JE, the aircraft media-nicknamed the "Lolita Express") document Bill Clinton as a passenger on at least 26 flight segments across the 2002-2003 period per the 6 May 2016 Fox News reporting on the court-released flight logs. The Wikipedia "Lolita Express" entry cites 27 flights to "at least a dozen international locations" across the same period.
- ** September 2002 Africa trip:** Bill Clinton flew on Epstein's aircraft to Africa with actors Kevin Spacey and Chris Tucker on an HIV/AIDS-project tour. The trip is on the documented public record.
- ** Secret Service record:** the 2017 Freedom of Information Act request established that there was no Secret Service documentary record of Clinton visiting Epstein's Little St. James island in the US Virgin Islands. The Epstein flight logs did not document Clinton flights to the island. The 2020 *Vanity Fair* reporting cited former Clinton advisor Doug Band as saying that Clinton had visited the island in January 2003; a Clinton spokesperson denied the visit and provided travel logs that did not include any mention of the visit. The aggregate conclusion: the documented flight-log record is the baseline; the contested island-visit record is on the post-2019 record subject to ongoing contestation.
- ** 2019 Clinton statement:** Clinton's 2019 spokesperson Angel Ureña stated that Clinton had "not spoken to Epstein in well over a decade, and has never been to Little Saint James, Epstein's ranch in New Mexico, or his residence in Florida." The statement reduced the Clinton acknowledgement of the Epstein flights to "four trips" on the aircraft.
- ** 2025 House Oversight Committee subpoenas:** in August 2025, the House Oversight Committee under Chairman James Comer issued subpoenas for testimony from Bill Clinton and Hillary Clinton regarding their Epstein interactions, along with request for "full, complete, unredacted Epstein Files." The subpoenas were the subject of contested compliance through the 2025-2026 period and have not, on the 2026 public record, produced public testimony from Bill or Hillary Clinton.

### Property holdings
- Chappaqua, NY, $1.7 million primary residence purchased 1999.
- Washington DC, $2.85 million townhouse purchased 2000.
- The post-2008 Clinton family property holdings are $4.5 million across the two primary residences per the public-record disclosures.

# Sources — Dossier 6: Anthony Charles Lynton Blair

## Biographical
- Born 6 May 1953, Edinburgh, Scotland.
- Father Leo Charles Lynton Blair (1923-2012), barrister and lecturer; mother Hazel Elizabeth Rosaleen Corscadden (1923-1975).
- Fettes College, Edinburgh, 1966-1971.
- St John's College, Oxford, BA in Jurisprudence, 1972-1975.
- Lincoln's Inn, called to the Bar 1976.
- Married Cherie Booth 29 March 1980 in Oxford.
- Member of Parliament for Sedgefield 1983-2007.
- Shadow Cabinet positions across 1984-1994, including Shadow Home Secretary 1992-1994.
- Leader of the Labour Party and Leader of the Opposition 21 July 1994 - 24 June 2007.
- Prime Minister of the United Kingdom 2 May 1997 - 27 June 2007.

## The 1997 election and the New Labour project

The 1997 general election produced the Labour Party's largest parliamentary majority in its history: 418 seats out of 659 (63.4% of the House of Commons), a 179-seat majority. The Conservative Party fell to 165 seats, the lowest since 1906. The Liberal Democrats took 46 seats. The Labour Party share of the national vote was 43.2%; the Conservative share was 30.7%.

The New Labour project was constructed across 1994-1997 by Blair, Gordon Brown, Peter Mandelson, and Alastair Campbell as the European counterpart to the Bill Clinton 1992 Democratic Leadership Council operation. The 1995 abolition of Clause IV of the Labour Party constitution — the clause that had since 1918 committed the Labour Party to "common ownership of the means of production, distribution and exchange" — was the symbolic moment at which the post-1994 Labour Party formally abandoned the socialist commitment in its constitution. The New Labour platform was: market-liberal economic policy ("the third way"); centrist social policy with selective progressive positioning on gay rights and women's representation; "tough on crime, tough on the causes of crime" criminal-justice rhetoric; constitutional reform (Scottish and Welsh devolution, House of Lords reform, the Human Rights Act 1998); and a continuing Atlanticist foreign-policy orientation that the subsequent Iraq War decision built on.

## The Iraq War — the documentary record

### The Bush-Blair political relationship 2001-2003
The Blair government's commitment to military action against Iraq was formed in the post-9/11 period through Blair's personal relationship with George W. Bush. The internal British government records made public in the Chilcot Inquiry establish the record of when Blair committed Britain to the invasion.

**The Crawford summit, 5-7 April 2002:** Blair met Bush at the Bush family ranch in Crawford, Texas. Intelligence officers who later gave evidence to the Chilcot Inquiry disclosed that Blair, in the days before the Crawford summit, "appeared to accept" the British intelligence assessment that Iraq possessed at most trivial amounts of weapons of mass destruction. Blair returned from Crawford a "changed man" and subsequently ordered the production of dossiers to "find the intelligence" that he wanted to use to justify going to war (per the 7 April 2013 *Independent on Sunday* reporting of in-camera Chilcot evidence).

**The 28 July 2002 Blair-to-Bush memorandum:** Blair wrote to Bush stating "I will be with you, whatever" — a commitment to military action made eight months before the parliamentary vote in March 2003 that authorised British participation. The memorandum is on the evidence released through the Chilcot Inquiry.

### The September 2002 dossier
On 24 September 2002, the Blair government published *Iraq's Weapons of Mass Destruction: The Assessment of the British Government* — the "September dossier." Blair's foreword stated that the Joint Intelligence Committee had established "beyond doubt" that Saddam Hussein's regime possessed WMD. The dossier included the claim that Iraq's military could deploy chemical and biological weapons "within 45 minutes of an order to use them."

The 45-minute claim produced the British tabloid front pages of 25 September 2002, including *The Sun*'s "Brits 45 mins from doom" headline.

### The February 2003 "dodgy dossier"
On 3 February 2003, the Blair government published a second dossier, *Iraq: Its Infrastructure of Concealment, Deception and Intimidation*. The portions of the dossier were subsequently revealed to have been plagiarised from a 2002 academic article by Ibrahim al-Marashi, a then-graduate student at the Monterey Institute of International Studies. The plagiarised text had been altered to strengthen the case for war. The publication was subsequently labelled the "dodgy dossier" in the British press.

### The Chilcot Inquiry findings (6 July 2016)
The Iraq Inquiry, chaired by Sir John Chilcot, was established by Gordon Brown in 2009 and reported on 6 July 2016 — seven years after its commencement. The report's principal findings on the evidence:

- **Intelligence assessment:** "Policy was made on the basis of flawed intelligence assessments. They were not challenged and should have been." The September 2002 dossier's claim that the JIC had established the WMD threat "beyond doubt" was, documented, "grounded in what Mr Blair believed rather than in the judgement which the JIC had actually reached." Iraq's capabilities were "presented with a certainty that was not justified."
- **Legal basis:** "In the absence of a majority in support of military action, we consider that the UK was, in fact, undermining the Security Council's authority." The legal argument for the invasion, on the documented Chilcot record, was inadequate.
- **War-readiness:** the UK military forces sent to Iraq were inadequately equipped for the post-invasion operation; the post-invasion planning was inadequate; the UK government had no plan for post-Saddam Iraq.
- **Diplomatic alternatives:** "Military action at that time was not a last resort." The diplomatic options had not been exhausted at the moment of the 18 March 2003 parliamentary vote.

### The casualty record
The 2003-2011 Iraq War produced documented casualty estimates ranging across:
- Coalition military deaths: 4,809 (US 4,431; UK 179; other coalition partners 199).
- Iraqi civilian deaths: Iraq Body Count documented 110,000-120,000 violent civilian deaths through 2011 from direct war-related violence. The 2006 *Lancet* survey by Burnham, Lafta, Doocy, and Roberts estimated 654,965 excess Iraqi deaths from violence through June 2006 (95% confidence interval 392,979 to 942,636). The Iraq Family Health Survey of 2008 estimated 151,000 violent deaths through June 2006. The 2013 PLoS Medicine study by Hagopian et al. estimated 461,000 excess deaths from war-related causes through 2011.

### The Major General Michael Laurie disclosure (May 2011)
In May 2011, Major General Michael Laurie — the British military intelligence officer at the Defence Intelligence Staff in 2002-2003 — gave previously secret evidence to the Chilcot Inquiry that was made public on 12 May 2011 by *The Guardian* (Richard Norton-Taylor reporting). Laurie's testimony: "We knew at the time that the purpose of the dossier was precisely to make a case for war, rather than setting out the available intelligence, and that to make the best out of sparse and inconclusive intelligence the wording was developed with care."

Laurie's testimony was the documented first-hand military-intelligence-officer contradiction of the Blair-and-Alastair-Campbell public position that the September dossier was a faithful representation of the intelligence assessment.

## Domestic policy

### Devolution
- Scotland Act 1998 (establishing the Scottish Parliament); Government of Wales Act 1998 (establishing the National Assembly for Wales); Northern Ireland Act 1998 (implementing the Good Friday Agreement of 10 April 1998, which is the diplomatic-political achievement of the Blair premiership).

### Constitutional and judicial reform
- Human Rights Act 1998 (incorporating the European Convention on Human Rights into UK law).
- House of Lords Act 1999 (removing hereditary peers from the House of Lords; 92 hereditary peers retained as a transitional arrangement that has continued through 2026).
- Constitutional Reform Act 2005 (establishing the UK Supreme Court, which took over the judicial functions of the House of Lords from 1 October 2009; abolishing the Lord Chancellor's judicial role).

### Education and the NHS
- Sure Start (1998); the expansion of the secondary-school academy programme from 2000; tuition fees for English universities (introduced at £1,000 in 1998, raised to £3,000 in 2004 by Higher Education Act 2004; raised to £9,000 by the Coalition government in 2010 under Cameron-Clegg-Willetts).
- NHS expansion across 1997-2007; NHS staffing levels increased; NHS Plan 2000; PFI hospital programme (Private Finance Initiative — the off-balance-sheet borrowing arrangement under which the Treasury paid private consortia to build hospitals that were subsequently leased back to the NHS at significant long-term cost premiums). The post-2010 academic-public-finance analysis of the PFI programme has documented the long-term cost to the NHS as £80 billion in cumulative payments for hospital infrastructure with a original capital value of £13 billion.

### Northern Ireland and the Good Friday Agreement
- The 10 April 1998 Good Friday Agreement (Belfast Agreement) between the British and Irish governments and the Northern Ireland political parties (with conspicuous Sinn Féin participation) ended the Northern Ireland Troubles that had lasted from 1968 to 1998. The agreement is the signature diplomatic-political achievement of the Blair premiership and is documented as the product of multi-year cross-party-and-cross-state negotiations involving the Clinton White House (George Mitchell as mediator), the Irish government under Bertie Ahern, the British Conservative-government precursor negotiations under John Major and Mo Mowlam, and the Sinn Féin leadership under Gerry Adams and Martin McGuinness.

## The post-premiership commercial portfolio

### The 27 June 2007 resignation and immediate appointments
- **27 June 2007:** Blair resigned as Prime Minister; Gordon Brown succeeded him.
- **27 June 2007:** Blair was appointed Special Envoy of the Quartet on the Middle East (the diplomatic representative of the US, the EU, the UN, and Russia on the Israeli-Palestinian peace process). He held the role through 27 May 2015. The post-resignation diplomatic appointment provided the structural access platform from which the subsequent commercial portfolio operated.

### JPMorgan Chase and Zurich Financial Services
- **January 2008:** Blair was appointed as a advisor to JPMorgan Chase. The fee was not publicly disclosed; subsequent reporting (The Financial Times; the 2012 *Nonprofit Quarterly* analysis) placed the fee at $4 million per year. Subsequent reporting (Powerbase; the *Spectator* analysis) suggested the fee was £2 million per year by 2012.
- **2008:** Blair was appointed as advisor to Zurich Financial Services on climate change. The fee was not publicly disclosed.
- **The 2008-2026 JPMorgan relationship:** the JPMorgan appointment was the cornerstone of the post-premiership commercial portfolio.

### Speaking fees
- The post-premiership Blair speaking fees: up to $250,000 for a 90-minute speech, with Blair reported in 2008 as the highest-paid speaker in the world. The 90,000 fee for a 20-minute talk at the Azerbaijan methanol-plant opening is documented (per the *Spectator* reporting on the Foreign Investors' Council Kazakhstan-and-Azerbaijan tour).

### Tony Blair Associates
- **February 2009:** Blair established Tony Blair Associates (TBA), the consultancy firm through which the post-premiership advisory contracts with foreign governments were operated. The 2009-2017 TBA portfolio included contracts with Kazakhstan, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Qatar, Colombia, the United Arab Emirates, Rwanda, Egypt, and other governments.
- **The Kazakhstan contract (2011-2017):**
- **October 2011:** Tony Blair Associates began advisory work for the Kazakhstan government under President Nursultan Nazarbayev. The initial contract value was £8 million per year (per the *Spectator* reporting). Subsequent reporting estimated the cumulative TBA payments from Kazakhstan at £20 million / $29.1 million across the multi-year arrangement (per the 2014 *Daily Mail* reporting documented by OCCRP). The monthly retainer is documented at £116,200 (~$170,000) in 2013, rising to £1.84 million ($2.68 million) per year by mid-2013; four personal Blair visits per year were guaranteed for £1.85 million.
- **The 16 December 2011 Zhanaozen massacre:** Kazakh police opened fire on striking oil workers in Zhanaozen, in the western Kazakhstan oil region. Casualty estimates: 15-17 confirmed killed, over 100 wounded (Kazakh government figures); independent sources estimated as many as 70 killed and 800 wounded. The massacre followed a lengthy strike by Kazakh oil workers at UzenMunayGas (KazMunayGas subsidiary) and Karazhanbasmunai (KazMunayGas/CITIC Group joint venture) demanding pay increases and the resignation of Nazarbayev.
- **The July 2012 Blair-to-Nazarbayev letter:** Blair sent Nazarbayev a advisory letter on "Office of Tony Blair" letterhead suggesting paragraphs for Nazarbayev's July 2012 University of Cambridge speech to address the Zhanaozen massacre. The Blair-drafted paragraphs urged Nazarbayev to "meet head on the Zhanaozen issue" and to position the massacre as a tragic incident that "should not obscure the enormous progress that Kazakhstan has made." Nazarbayev delivered the Blair-drafted material in the Cambridge speech, lightly edited. The letter was made public by the *Daily Telegraph* in August 2014.
- **The parallel Blair-to-Baroness Ashton letter:** Blair approved a letter to the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs Catherine Ashton positioning Kazakhstan as "a remarkable success story" and downplaying the Zhanaozen massacre.
- **The Kazakhstan portfolio cumulative payment:** the 2014 *Daily Mail* reporting estimated the cumulative TBA-Kazakhstan payments at over £20 million / $29.1 million across the multi-year arrangement.
- **The Saudi Arabia contract:**
- The Saudi Arabian government engaged Tony Blair Associates as an advisor across the 2010-2018 period. The 21 July 2018 *Daily Telegraph* reporting documented a £9 million Saudi-government contract with Blair's organisation.
- **The 2009-2016 personal-income record:** the *Financial Times* 2012 estimate of Blair's annual personal income from speeches and consultancy was $30 million.

### Tony Blair Institute for Global Change

- **Founded:** 2016 (incorporated 2016 from the reserves of the earlier Tony Blair Foundation and Tony Blair Faith Foundation operations; trade name adopted 2017).
- **Mission (per TBI public-facing materials):** advise politicians, governments, and businesses on strategy and policy; focus on the role of technology in politics, including artificial intelligence and digital ID.
- **Funding through 2024:**
- **Saudi Arabia:** documented £9 million Saudi-government contract reported by the *Daily Telegraph* 21 July 2018.
- **US Department of State:** confirmed donor (Wikipedia; TBI public disclosures).
- **Larry Ellison / Larry Ellison Foundation:** the dominant funder. Per the April 2025 *Guardian* reporting and the 2025 ProPublica filings: Ellison Foundation donations to TBI of $5 million in 2018, $10 million in 2019, $10 million in 2020, $34 million in 2021, $44 million in 2022, $52 million in 2023, with $163 million further pledged through 2027. The cumulative Ellison commitment to TBI through 2025 is $348 million per the January 2026 *Drey Dossier* analysis, or £257-272 million per the 2024-2025 *Times* and *New Statesman* reporting (the currency conversions vary).
- **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation:** documented donor per the 2023 TBI accounts.
- **United Arab Emirates:** TBI client per the Wikipedia entry.
- **Azerbaijan:** paid TBI work in 2024 around the COP29 conference per the 2025 *Guardian* reporting.

- ** 2023 financials:** $140-145 million annual revenue per TBI public reporting; 900-1,000 staff projected by end of 2024; advised more than 40 governments by 2024.
- ** post-Khashoggi position:** TBI continued paid work for Saudi Arabia after the 2 October 2018 assassination of Saudi dissident journalist Jamal Khashoggi at the Saudi consulate in Istanbul, per the 2024-2025 *Guardian*, *Times*, and *New Statesman* reporting.

### The Oracle / Ellison relationship and the Lighthouse Reports investigation

The January 2026 *Lighthouse Reports* investigation (with UK partner *Democracy for Sale*), based on 29 current-and-former-TBI-staff interviews across four months, documented the structural relationship between TBI and Oracle Corporation:

- TBI staff and Oracle executives held joint strategy meetings at TBI's One Bartholomew Place London headquarters; TBI employees were hosted at Oracle's Austin Texas headquarters; "joint retreats at Ellison properties in the US" preceded the public-record Oracle-TBI partnership.
- TBI employees described their work as "explicitly promoting Oracle's services and acting as a 'sales engine'" to governments across the Global South.
- The February 2025 Dubai World Governments Summit Blair-Ellison interview produced the Ellison statement that the NHS data infrastructure was "fragmented"; two weeks later, TBI published *Governing in the Age of AI: Building Britain's National Data Library*, echoing the Ellison framing of the UK data infrastructure as "fragmented and unfit for purpose."
- Awo Ablo sat on the board of both TBI and Oracle.
- Oracle's UK public-sector revenue across 2022-2025: £1.1 billion per the Tussell procurement analytics figures.
- Oracle ran portions of UK central government financial-and-planning software for the Department for Work and Pensions, the Ministry of Justice, the Department for Environment Food and Rural Affairs, the Cabinet Office, the Home Office, HM Treasury, and the Ministry of Defence (per the 2023 *Register* reporting).

### Sources

## Cross-references

# Sources — Dossier 7: Nancy Patricia D'Alesandro Pelosi

## Biographical
- Born 26 March 1940, Baltimore, Maryland, as Nancy Patricia D'Alesandro.
- Father Thomas Ludwig John D'Alesandro Jr. (1903-1987): US Congressman 1939-1947 (Maryland's 3rd district); Mayor of Baltimore 1947-1959.
- Brother Thomas D'Alesandro III (1929-2019): Mayor of Baltimore 1967-1971.
- Trinity College Washington DC (now Trinity Washington University), BA in political science, 1962.
- Married Paul Frank Pelosi 7 September 1963 in Baltimore. Paul Pelosi born 15 April 1940, businessman in real estate, private equity, and venture capital based in San Francisco.
- Five children: Nancy Corinne, Christine, Jacqueline, Paul Jr., Alexandra.
- Member of the United States House of Representatives for California's 5th, 8th, then 12th and now 11th district (boundary redistricting), 7 June 1987 - present (continuing through January 2027 when current term expires).
- Chair of the California Democratic Party 1981-1983.
- Chair of the Democratic National Convention 1984 Compliance Review Commission.
- House Democratic Whip 2002-2003.
- House Minority Leader 2003-2007 and 2011-2019.
- Speaker of the House 2007-2011 (110th and 111th Congresses) and 2019-2023 (116th and 117th Congresses). First woman to serve as Speaker.
- Speaker Emerita from 2023.
- Announced 6 November 2025 that she will not seek re-election; term ends 3 January 2027.

### Family background and the D'Alesandro Baltimore inheritance

The D'Alesandro family was a Baltimore Italian-American Democratic political family. Thomas D'Alesandro Jr. served three terms as a US Congressman (1939-1947) and three terms as Mayor of Baltimore (1947-1959). Thomas D'Alesandro III served one term as Mayor of Baltimore (1967-1971), succeeding the older D'Alesandro tradition. Nancy Pelosi was the youngest of seven children and the only daughter. She was raised in Baltimore's Little Italy neighbourhood, in the family home above her father's mayoral campaign office. She volunteered in her father's political campaigns from childhood. The D'Alesandro family is documented as a Baltimore Democratic political machine family with documented Mafia-adjacent associations through Thomas D'Alesandro Jr.'s tenure as mayor (the documented 1953 FBI file establishes the Baltimore D'Alesandro-Sabatino-Frank-Costello associations the FBI investigated).

### The San Francisco transition

Nancy Pelosi and Paul Pelosi moved to San Francisco in 1969 after Paul Pelosi's investment-banking-and-real-estate business required relocation. Nancy Pelosi raised the five children through the 1970s while volunteering in California Democratic Party operations. She was Chair of the California Democratic Party from 1981 to 1983. She was selected to chair the Democratic National Convention's 1984 Compliance Review Commission, the intra-party adjudication body for the 1984 nomination process. In June 1987 she won the special election for California's 5th congressional district seat after the death of Congresswoman Sala Burton, who had endorsed Pelosi as her successor from her deathbed. Pelosi has held the San Francisco congressional seat continuously since 1987.

## The Speaker tenures

### First Speaker tenure 2007-2011 (110th and 111th Congresses)
- Speaker elected 4 January 2007 after the 2006 Democratic House majority following the Republican 1994-2006 majority.
- The 2008 financial-crisis legislative response was passed under the Pelosi speakership: the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) signed by George W. Bush on 3 October 2008 ($700 billion bank-bailout authorisation); the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 signed by Obama 17 February 2009 ($831 billion stimulus); the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act signed 21 July 2010.
- The 2010 Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) — the signature Pelosi-Obama domestic-policy legislative achievement — signed 23 March 2010 after the multi-year congressional negotiations and the House reconciliation process Pelosi managed.
- The 2010 midterm elections produced a 63-seat Democratic loss in the House and the Republican majority that ended the first Pelosi speakership.

### Second Speaker tenure 2019-2023 (116th and 117th Congresses)
- Speaker elected 3 January 2019 after the 2018 Democratic House majority following the 2010-2018 Republican majority.
- The first Trump impeachment (December 2019): Pelosi initiated the impeachment inquiry on 24 September 2019 over the July 2019 Trump-Zelensky call. House impeachment vote 18 December 2019 on two articles (abuse of power; obstruction of Congress); Senate acquittal 5 February 2020.
- COVID-19 legislative response under the Pelosi speakership: CARES Act ($2.2 trillion) signed by Trump 27 March 2020; American Rescue Plan Act ($1.9 trillion) signed by Biden 11 March 2021.
- The 6 January 2021 Capitol attack: covered in detail below.
- The second Trump impeachment (January 2021): House impeachment vote 13 January 2021 on one article (incitement of insurrection); Senate acquittal 13 February 2021.
- The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act signed November 2021; the CHIPS and Science Act signed August 2022; the Inflation Reduction Act signed August 2022.
- Pelosi stepped down as Democratic Leader 17 November 2022 after the 2022 midterm elections; she became Speaker Emerita from January 2023.

## The 6 January 2021 Capitol attack record

The documentary record across the 2021-2024 period includes the HBO documentary footage shot by Alexandra Pelosi (Nancy Pelosi's daughter, a documentary filmmaker) on 5-7 January 2021. The footage was the subject of multiple 2024 releases — first to Congress, then to the public via CNN, NBC, and the House Administration Subcommittee on Oversight under Chairman Barry Loudermilk.

### The documented Pelosi statements on the 6 January record
- On 6 January 2021, during the Capitol evacuation, Pelosi said to her chief of staff: "Why weren't the National Guard there to begin with... I take responsibility for not having them just prepare for more. Because it's stupid." She continued: "How many times did the members ask, 'Are we prepared? Are we prepared?' We're not prepared for the worst." And: "Shame on us... I'm suspicious of them and their motivations, tell you the truth."
- On the phone call from Pelosi's evacuation vehicle to acting Defense Secretary Christopher Miller (jointly with Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer), Pelosi and Schumer requested National Guard deployment.
- On the phone call to Virginia Governor Ralph Northam, Pelosi requested Virginia National Guard deployment to DC.
- On the morning of 7 January 2021, in the car back to the Capitol, Pelosi told her aide Drew Hammill: "I think our focus has to be on the president. Let's not divert ourselves." When Hammill noted the press focus on Capitol Police Chief Steven Sund's resignation, Pelosi said: "I don't want to have it on par with the insurrection and impeachment and the rest of that."
- On 7 January 2021 at 3:30 AM, Pelosi said of Trump: "I just feel sick about what he did to the Capitol and the country today. He's gotta pay a price for that. There's a domestic enemy in the White House. And let's not mince words about this."

### The record on Capitol security on 6 January 2021
- The Capitol Police Board is the body responsible for Capitol security and the National Guard request authority on the Capitol grounds. The Capitol Police Board includes the House Sergeant-at-Arms, the Senate Sergeant-at-Arms, and the Architect of the Capitol. The Speaker has authority over House operations and House Sergeant-at-Arms appointment but does not directly command National Guard deployment.
- The National Guard reports operationally to the US Army and the Air Force under the President.
- The pre-6 January 2021 Capitol Police Chief Steven Sund's subsequent public testimony (in his January 2022 book and in his 2024 Subcommittee on Oversight testimony) was that he requested National Guard reinforcement in the days before 6 January 2021 and that the House Sergeant-at-Arms Paul Irving and the Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Michael Stenger rebuffed the request. Sund resigned 7 January 2021 after Pelosi called for his resignation; Irving and Stenger also resigned.
- The House Select Committee on the 6 January Attack — chaired by Bennie Thompson with Liz Cheney as vice-chair — produced the December 2022 final report that focused on Trump's role and did not focus on the Capitol Police Board's pre-6-January-2021 preparation failures. The House Administration Subcommittee on Oversight under Chairman Loudermilk from 2023 has documented what the Select Committee declined to focus on.
- The position of House Republicans through 2024 has been that the Pelosi-Speaker role in the House operations and the House Sergeant-at-Arms appointment makes Pelosi the responsible figure for the Capitol security failures of 6 January 2021. The position of House Democrats and the Select Committee was that Trump's incitement was the cause of the 6 January attack and that the responsibility analysis must focus on Trump.

The terminal assessment: Pelosi's own contemporaneous statements on 6 January 2021 — as documented in the HBO footage — included the phrase "I take responsibility for not having them just prepare for more." The subsequent public Pelosi position from January 2021 onward was that Trump was solely responsible for 6 January and that the Capitol security analysis should not be allowed to "divert" from the Trump-incitement narrative. Both positions are documented. The Capitol Police Board institutional structure means Pelosi was not the sole responsible figure for the National Guard pre-deployment decisions; the Pelosi-Speaker authority over House operations means she was not without responsibility either.

## The Pelosi-family commercial trading record

### The STOCK Act framework
The Stop Trading on Congressional Knowledge (STOCK) Act of 2012 was signed by Obama on 4 April 2012. The Act requires members of Congress to disclose their transactions (and the transactions of their spouses and children) within 45 days of execution, in value ranges rather than exact figures, through Periodic Transaction Reports (PTRs) filed with the House Clerk and the Senate Secretary.

The STOCK Act does not prohibit members of Congress from trading; it only requires disclosure of the trades. The 2022 bipartisan effort to prohibit congressional stock trading included the House Administration Committee proposals from progressive (Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Elizabeth Warren) and conservative (Chip Roy, Josh Hawley) figures. The 2022 effort was defeated in the House when Pelosi's office declined to bring the proposals to a floor vote. The 2025 PELOSI Act (renamed by Senate Republicans from the earlier bipartisan proposal) is pending through 2026.

### The Pelosi-family documented trading record
- The 1987 Pelosi-family portfolio value: $610,000-$785,000 per the 2025 *New York Post* analysis of Pelosi's 1987 House financial disclosures.
- The 2024 Pelosi-family portfolio value: estimated $130 million per the 2025 *New York Post* analysis (a return of 16,900% across the 1987-2024 period).
- The 2024 Pelosi-family portfolio return: 54% per the *New York Post* analysis, against the S&P 500's 25% return in the same year. The 2014-2026 trading volume: $213 million across 201 transactions per the Gainify analysis of the STOCK Act disclosures. The 2022-2025 trading volume: $59 million per Capitol Trades.
- The key documented Pelosi-family trade: December 2024 Paul Pelosi exercised 50,000 Nvidia call options purchased in 2023 at a strike price of $12 per share; the Nvidia share price at the exercise was $135. The gross gain from the single exercise was $6 million.
- The 2025 Tempus AI Paul Pelosi options purchase (17 January 2025) was followed by the Tempus AI share-price rise from $35.15 to $89.44 within one month — the "Pelosi Effect" pattern that the 2022-2026 congressional-trading-tracker community has documented.
- Other 2024-2025 Pelosi-family trades that have produced public attention: Broadcom call exercise June 2025 ($1-5 million); Apple stock transfer of at least $100,000 from Paul Pelosi to a Catholic university on 22 October 2025.

### The public response
- The Pelosi position has consistently been that Pelosi herself does not personally execute trades, that Paul Pelosi is the investor responsible for the trades, and that she has no prior knowledge of or involvement in the trades. The Pelosi office statement: "Speaker Pelosi does not own any stocks, and she has no prior knowledge or subsequent involvement in any transactions."
- The 2023 NANC fund (Subversive Unusual Whales Democratic Trading ETF) tracks Democratic congressional trades and the parallel Subversive Unusual Whales Republican Trading ETF tracks Republican congressional trades. The NANC fund net assets in 2025: $263 million per the *CNN Politics* reporting. The NANC fund 2024 return: 30%+ (outperforming the S&P 500). The comprehensive conclusion: the Pelosi-family trading record is on the documented public record; the interpretive question of whether the 16,900% return reflects privileged-information advantage or independent-market acumen is contested; the congressional-trading-tracker community has built a multi-million-dollar industry on the assumption that Pelosi's trades reflect information advantage.

## The 2024 Biden-withdrawal-pressure role

Following the 27 June 2024 Trump-Biden debate at which Biden's cognitive decline became publicly evident (Dossier 1 covers the Biden side of the record), the Democratic Party leadership including Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, and Pelosi (then Speaker Emerita) conducted the multi-week pressure operation that produced Biden's 21 July 2024 withdrawal.

### The Pelosi role per the record
- 10 July 2024: Pelosi told MSNBC's *Morning Joe*: "We're all encouraging him to make that decision, because time is running short." The framing that the decision was "up to" Biden — repeated through the subsequent two weeks — was the public-facing position.
- Mid-July 2024 phone call (per NBC News reporting 23 July 2024): Pelosi told Biden in a private phone call that he could "lose so badly to Donald Trump that he could damage Democrats down ballot and possibly cost them control of the House and the Senate." Per the NBC reporting, Pelosi asked Biden adviser Mike Donilon to get on the call to talk through the polling data when Biden became defensive about the polling.
- Per the subsequent NBC and CNN reporting, when the Trump-assassination-attempt momentum of 13 July 2024 appeared to stall the Biden-withdrawal pressure, the Pelosi-aligned Democratic figures (Rep. Adam Schiff, Rep. Jared Huffman) brought the story back to the public attention through 17-19 July 2024 statements.
- 21 July 2024: Biden announced his withdrawal from the 2024 race.
- The Pelosi public position from August 2024 onward (in the CBS *Sunday Morning* interview with Lesley Stahl) was that she did not lead a pressure campaign and "did not call one person." The subsequent reporting documented the multiple phone calls between Pelosi and Biden across the mid-July 2024 period. The documented determination: the documented record establishes that Pelosi was the central figure in the Biden-withdrawal pressure operation, and that her public position denying the role is contradicted by the multiple-source NBC, CNN, and ABC reporting.

## The November 2025 retirement announcement

On 6 November 2025 — two days after the passage of the California Proposition 50 redistricting ballot measure (which Pelosi had personally raised $40 million in fundraising for and which redrew the California congressional map to favour Democrats ahead of the 2026 midterms) — Pelosi released a video announcement that she would not seek re-election. The term ends 3 January 2027.

The Proposition 50 redistricting measure is the final Pelosi political-class operation of her congressional career: the California congressional-map redraw is projected to flip up to five House seats to Democrats in the 2026 midterms, partially offsetting the Republican-state 2024-2025 redistricting operations in Texas, North Carolina, and other Republican-controlled states.

Pelosi will be 86 years old on 26 March 2026; she will be 86 at the conclusion of her term in January 2027.

# Sources — Dossier 8: Charles Ellis Schumer

## Biographical
- Born 23 November 1950, Brooklyn, New York, as Charles Ellis Schumer.
- Father Abraham Schumer (1923-2017): operated a small exterminating business in Brooklyn.
- Mother Selma Rosen Schumer (1928-2018): homemaker.
- Younger brother Robert Schumer (b. 1953): partner at Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP — the major Wall Street law firm — with practice in mergers and acquisitions.
- Cousin Amy Schumer (b. 1981): American comedian and actress.
- James Madison High School, Brooklyn, 1967. Perfect 1600 SAT score in 1967.
- Harvard College, BA in social studies, 1971 (graduated *magna cum laude*).
- Harvard Law School, JD, 1974.
- New York State Bar admission 1975.
- Married Iris Weinshall (b. 1953) in 1980. Iris Weinshall is the Chief Operating Officer of the New York Public Library; prior roles include Commissioner of the New York City Department of Transportation under Mayor Rudy Giuliani 2000-2007 and Vice Chancellor of the City University of New York.
- Two daughters: Jessica Schumer and Alison Schumer.

## Political-career timeline
- New York State Assembly, 45th district (Brooklyn), 1975-1981. Elected at age 23, the youngest member of the New York Assembly since 1817.
- US House of Representatives, New York's 16th (1981-1983) and 9th (1983-1999) congressional districts.
- US Senate, New York, since 3 January 1999. Currently serving his fifth six-year term (re-elected 1998, 2004, 2010, 2016, 2022). Term ends January 2029.
- Senate Democratic Conference Vice Chair, 2007-2017.
- Senate Democratic Leader, 3 January 2017 - present.
- Senate Minority Leader 2017-2021 (115th and 116th Congresses).
- Senate Majority Leader 20 January 2021 - 3 January 2025 (117th and 118th Congresses).
- Senate Minority Leader 3 January 2025 - present (119th Congress).
- The first New Yorker to be named Senate Minority Leader (2017) and the first New Yorker to be Senate Majority Leader (2021). The first Jewish Senate Majority Leader.

## The Wall Street donor relationship — documented record

The Schumer Wall Street fundraising record is on the documented public record across the 1999-2026 Senate career.

### Senate Banking and Finance Committee positions
- Schumer served on the Senate Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs Committee from 1999 — the committee with jurisdiction over the banking, financial-services, and insurance industries.
- Schumer served on the Senate Finance Committee from 2009 — the committee with jurisdiction over the tax-and-trade policy.
- In 2015, Schumer passed on the opportunity to become Ranking Member of the Banking Committee in favour of Sherrod Brown (a bank critic), allowing Brown the position while Schumer preserved the position for the subsequent Democratic Leader candidacy.

### The 1999-2024 documented Wall Street fundraising
- Between his 1999 Senate ascension and his 2004 re-election, Schumer raised $27.5 million per the *Jacobin* analysis of his FEC filings.
- By 2005 Schumer was among the top recipients of fundraising across accounting, real estate, and commercial banks, and was the number-one recipient of tobacco-industry money per the contemporaneous Politico and *Jacobin* reporting.
- By 2008 Schumer had received more money from the securities and investment industry than any member of Congress other than John Kerry.
- In 2009 Schumer received 15% of all Wall Street political donations — close to double the next-highest recipient and five times the highest-receiving Republican senator. The 15% figure was reported by Politico in November 2009 and has been the basis for the "the senator from Wall Street" media nickname.
- Schumer's five largest career donors per the 2025 *Wall Street On Parade* analysis: Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, JPMorgan Chase (three of the five largest), and Paul Weiss (Schumer's brother Robert's law firm).
- The 2019 Senate Majority PAC — the Democratic-Senate-leadership-aligned super-PAC tied to Schumer — raised a record $61 million for the 2020 cycle per *City & State New York*.

### Documented votes on financial regulation
- 2010 Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform Act: Schumer voted yes. The 2010 Brown-Kaufman amendment to break up the too-big-to-fail banks: Schumer was one of 27 Democrats who voted no.
- 2010 Carried Interest Tax Reform: Schumer killed the Democratic effort to double the tax on the private-equity and hedge-fund carried-interest profits after the industry contributed $1 million to Schumer per the *Jacobin* analysis. The Senate did not pass the reform.
- 2014 Elizabeth Warren-Stanley Fischer Federal Reserve confirmation dispute: when Warren expressed concerns about confirming former Citigroup executive Stanley Fischer to the Vice Chair of the Federal Reserve, Schumer told her that "three years at Citibank, I think, should be an asset rather than a liability."
- 2014 Cromnibus appropriations bill rider on Dodd-Frank derivatives push-out (Section 716): the Citigroup-drafted rollback of Dodd-Frank that allowed FDIC-insured banks to trade derivatives — Schumer voted for the cromnibus that contained the rider.

### The 2024-2025 Trump-Schumer overlap and the Paul Weiss connection
- 14 March 2025 government shutdown vote: Schumer pivoted from his 12 March 2025 public position opposing the Republican Continuing Resolution to a 14 March 2025 vote in favour of the Republican CR, providing the 60th Senate vote that prevented the government shutdown. The pivot was the subject of public Democratic criticism.
- The *Wall Street On Parade* 17 March 2025 analysis of the Schumer pivot noted: (a) the four megabank-uninsured-deposit total of $3.2 trillion that would have been the subject of depositor flight under a shutdown; (b) the 14 March 2025 Trump Executive Order stripping the Paul Weiss law firm of its security clearances — the Executive Order targeting Schumer's brother Robert's firm. The closing verdict: the 14 March 2025 Schumer pivot occurred on the same day the Trump Executive Order targeted Paul Weiss; the subsequent 20 March 2025 Paul Weiss settlement with the Trump administration included a $40 million pro-bono commitment to the Trump-aligned causes; the Schumer-Paul Weiss-brother relationship is documented; the coincidence of timing is documented.

## The AIPAC alignment and the 14 March 2024 Israel speech

Schumer is documented as a lifelong AIPAC-aligned pro-Israel elected official and the highest-ranking Jewish elected official in American government. The Schumer position on Israel across the Senate career has been pro-Likud through the 2010-2024 period.

### The 14 March 2024 Senate floor speech
On 14 March 2024 — five months into the post-October-7 Israel-Gaza war — Schumer delivered a 40-minute speech on the Senate floor titled "Israel After October 7." The speech was the most prominent public Democratic-Party-leadership criticism of the Netanyahu government to that point. Key quoted passages:

- "The Netanyahu coalition no longer fits the needs of Israel after October 7. The world has changed, radically, since then, and the Israeli people are being stifled right now by a governing vision that is stuck in the past."
- "Five months into this conflict, it is clear that Israelis need to take stock of the situation and ask: must we change course?"
- "At this critical juncture, I believe a new election is the only way to allow for a healthy and open decision-making process about the future of Israel."
- Schumer named four obstacles to peace: "Netanyahu, Hamas and extremist Palestinians, radical right-wing Israelis [naming Smotrich and Ben-Gvir], and Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas."
- Schumer described Smotrich and Ben-Gvir as "the worst examples" of right-wing Israeli radical extremism.
- Schumer expressed continued opposition to calls for a immediate and permanent ceasefire and continued support for Israeli weapons supply and US diplomatic-and-military support.
- Schumer rejected a one-state binational solution as "a bridge too far to accept" that would put Jewish Israelis in danger.

### responses
- The Netanyahu Likud party response: "Israel is an independent and proud democracy that elected Prime Minister Netanyahu, not a banana republic."
- The AIPAC response: "Israel is an independent democracy that decides for itself when elections are held and chooses its own leaders. America must continue to stand with our ally Israel and ensure it has the time and resources it needs to win this war. Hamas bears sole responsibility for this conflict."
- The Mitch McConnell response on the Senate floor: "Israel deserves an ally that acts like one" and foreign observers "ought to refrain from weighing in."
- The Yair Lapid response: Schumer's call for elections was "proof that one by one [Netanyahu] is losing Israel's biggest supporters in the US."

The systematic conclusion: the 14 March 2024 speech was a calibrated public-position shift by the most Jewish American elected official from the unconditional pro-Netanyahu position of the 2010-2023 period to a anti-Netanyahu-government-while-still-pro-Israel position. The speech did not propose conditioning American weapons supply on Israeli conduct; the speech maintained US weapons supply; the speech named Smotrich and Ben-Gvir as obstacles to peace but did not name the IDF conduct under the Netanyahu war cabinet. The speech was the product of the multi-month Democratic Party internal management of the post-October-7 grassroots Democratic-voter-and-college-campus pressure on the Biden administration's Israel policy.

## The 13 July 2024 Biden withdrawal meeting

On the morning of Saturday 13 July 2024 — sixteen days after the 27 June 2024 Trump-Biden debate at which Biden's cognitive decline had become publicly evident — Schumer drove four hours from Brooklyn to Biden's Rehoboth Beach, Delaware, beach house for a 45-minute private meeting in which he urged Biden to withdraw from the 2024 race.

### The documented record
Per the 17 January 2025 *New York Times* reporting based on Schumer's own subsequent account:

- Schumer told Biden: "If you run and you lose to Trump, and we lose the Senate, and we don't get back the House, that 50 years of amazing, beautiful work goes out the window. But worse — you go down in American history as one of the darkest figures."
- Schumer told Biden: "If I were you, I wouldn't run, and I'm urging you not to run."
- Schumer told Biden the 2024 race produced a 5% chance of beating Trump per Senate-Democratic-Conference internal polling.
- Biden interrupted twice to ask the same question: "Do you really think Kamala can win?" Schumer responded that he did not know but that Harris had a far better chance than Biden.
- Schumer told Biden the message had originally been intended for Obama to deliver, but Obama had told Schumer that Obama's "fragile relationship" with Biden (dating to the 2016 Hillary Clinton endorsement and subsequent friction) would mean Biden would not listen to Obama, so Schumer would have to deliver the message.
- The Biden response per the NYT reporting: "You've got bigger balls than anyone I've ever met."
- Schumer left the meeting and reportedly broke down in tears.

The meeting occurred shortly before the 13 July 2024 Trump assassination attempt at Butler, Pennsylvania. The assassination attempt produced the 24-hour public-attention shift that the Schumer-Pelosi-Jeffries withdrawal-pressure operation regrouped around in the subsequent week.

### The public framing 13 July - 17 January
- 13 July 2024 Schumer office statement: "I sat with President Biden this afternoon in Delaware; we had a good meeting."
- 17 July 2024 ABC News reporting (Jonathan Karl): Schumer "privately urged Biden to step aside" in the 13 July meeting.
- 17 July 2024 Schumer office response: "Unless ABC's source is Chuck Schumer or President Joe Biden the reporting is idle speculation. Leader Schumer conveyed the views of his caucus directly to President Biden on Saturday."
- 21 July 2024: Biden announced withdrawal.
- 13 February 2024 Schumer public position (predating the 13 July meeting): "I talk to President Biden, you know, regularly, sometimes several times in a week... His mental acuity is great. It's fine. It's as good as it's been over the years." Concerns about Biden's physical-and-mental condition were "right-wing propaganda."
- 17 January 2025: NYT publication of the Schumer-disclosed account of the 13 July meeting.
- Subsequent NBC News interview with Kristen Welker: Schumer denied that he and other Democrats had "misled" Americans about Biden's mental acuity.

The weighted conclusion: Schumer's 13 February 2024 public statement that Biden's mental acuity was "great" and that the concerns were "right-wing propaganda" is documented alongside Schumer's 13 July 2024 private statement that Biden could "go down in American history as one of the darkest figures" if he ran. Both statements are documented. The interval between the two statements was five months. The question of whether Schumer was deceiving the American public on 13 February 2024 or whether the Biden cognitive decline accelerated across the intervening five months is documented subject to contested interpretation; the multi-source reporting in the NYT, WSJ, and Tapper-and-Thompson *Original Sin* (May 2025) has documented the Biden cognitive decline as the multi-year process that the Democratic Party leadership including Schumer had been aware of across the 2022-2024 period.

## The 14 March 2025 government-shutdown pivot

On 14 March 2025 Schumer voted in favour of the Republican Continuing Resolution (CR) that funded the federal government through September 2025. The CR had been opposed by the majority of Senate Democrats; Schumer provided the decisive 60th vote that ended the Democratic filibuster and allowed the CR to pass.

The pivot occurred two days after Schumer's 12 March 2025 public statement that Democrats would "stand firm" against the Republican CR. The public Schumer explanation for the pivot was: "As bad as passing the CR is, allowing Donald Trump to take even more power via a government shutdown is a far worse option."

The parallel events of 14 March 2025:
- The Trump Executive Order stripping the Paul Weiss law firm of its security clearances was signed 14 March 2025. Paul Weiss is the firm of Schumer's brother Robert.
- The subsequent 20 March 2025 Paul Weiss settlement with the Trump administration included a $40 million pro-bono commitment to Trump-aligned causes.

The 14 March 2025 Schumer pivot was the subject of public Democratic criticism from progressive Democratic figures (Elizabeth Warren; Bernie Sanders; Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez) and from Democratic primary-electorate polling that showed 60%+ Democratic-voter opposition to the pivot.
