# Bibliography

This consolidated bibliography lists the principal published works, archival sources, and primary documents referenced in *Behind the Performance: The Demon They Call the State*. Per-chapter source listings appear in the Sources section above and contain the full per-claim verification record with clickable URLs where digital sources are available. The bibliography here gathers the distinct published works for the reader who wants a single alphabetical reference list.

Government documents, regulatory filings, court rulings, treaty texts, and contemporary press articles are cited inline at the per-chapter Sources level with clickable URLs where available. The bibliography below focuses on the books, scholarly monographs, peer-reviewed works, and biographical works that constitute the principal published-source apparatus of the volume.

---

## Per-chapter Sources

The per-chapter Sources sections (organised by dossier and chapter, immediately above this Bibliography) contain the full per-claim verification record. Each source entry is cited with publication name, date, and clickable URL where available. The Bibliography above is for the reader who wants a single alphabetised reference list of the distinct published works that the dossiers and chapters draw on; it is not a replacement for the per-chapter Sources sections.

Verification was conducted through web search and primary-document lookup during the drafting of each chapter and dossier across 2025-2026. Wherever a claim is contested between sources, the chapter text marks the contest; the per-chapter Sources sections record what was reviewed.

The reader must evaluate the evidence directly. The Bibliography catalogues the published works that informed the volume's documentary record; the reader is invited to examine any of them independently.

# Glossary

A reader-facing reference for the principal terms used in *Behind the Performance: The Demon They Call the State*. Terms are defined at first encounter in the text; the Glossary collects them in one place for the reader who wants to look a term up without flipping back. The Glossary covers terms used in this volume and a small number of terms that recur across *The Last Mind* series.

---

**Adani Group**. The Indian conglomerate controlled by Gautam Adani. Documented contemporary integration with the Modi BJP administration. Discussed in the Modi dossier and the RSS-VHP institutional dossier.

**AIPAC (American Israel Public Affairs Committee)**. The senior US lobby for the Israel-US political-economic-military relationship. Dossier 17 documents its operational architecture.

**Anthropic**. US AI company founded January 2021 by Dario and Daniela Amodei and seven former OpenAI co-founders. Maker of the Claude AI products. The volume was produced in operational symbiosis with Claude Opus 4.7. Anthropic is dossiered in Volume One Chapter 21 and referenced across the Vol 2 AI-policy capture analysis (Part XIII Chapter 3, String 10).

**Atlantic Council**. Washington-based foreign-policy think tank. Heavy defence-contractor funding (Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, others). Referenced as one of the senior US foreign-policy-establishment architectures.

**Bank for International Settlements (BIS)**. Founded 17 May 1930. The central bank of central banks, headquartered in Basel, Switzerland. Dossier 76 documents its contemporary role in the Bretton Woods architecture.

**Bilderberg Meeting**. Annual private meeting founded in 1954. Roughly 120-150 senior figures from business, politics, finance, and media. Operates under the Chatham House Rule. Referenced across the dossier chapters and named as one of the contemporary convening architectures in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**BJP (Bharatiya Janata Party)**. The Hindu-nationalist political party governing India under Narendra Modi. Discussed in Dossiers 21-23.

**BlackRock**. The world's largest asset manager. Founded 1988 by Larry Fink and seven co-founders. Roughly $11 trillion AUM. The BlackRock-Biden pipeline is the first named String in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**Bretton Woods Architecture**. The post-WWII international monetary settlement signed 22 July 1944 at Bretton Woods, New Hampshire. Created the IMF, the World Bank, and the dollar-as-reserve-currency arrangement. Untethered from gold by the Nixon administration on 15 August 1971. Dossier 76 documents the contemporary architecture.

**Capital**. In the volume's vocabulary, the four-century debt-money mechanism Volume One documents. The Devil. The source-and-root issue from which the state apparatus operates as one of the masks.

**Chenoweth-Stephan dataset**. The civil-resistance research database compiled by Erica Chenoweth and Maria Stephan documenting historical disruption-of-political-class movements. The dataset is the empirical foundation for the Part XIV Chapter 3 disruption-trajectory projection.

**Citizens United v. FEC (2010)**. The US Supreme Court decision that opened US elections to unlimited corporate political spending. Discussed in the SCOTUS dossier and across the contemporary US political-class chapters.

**Claude**. The AI assistant product made by Anthropic. The volume was produced in symbiosis with Claude Opus 4.7. Multiple model versions exist; Opus 4.7 is the volume's documented operational counterpart.

**Conspiracy theory**. A term the volume engages directly in the Prologue. The volume's position: a conspiracy is when people agree, in private, to do a thing that harms others, and arrange the world so the harm continues and the agreement is hidden. By that definition the documented public record of the contemporary political class is not theory; it is record.

**Council on Foreign Relations (CFR)**. The senior US foreign-policy establishment institution. Referenced as one of the contemporary convening architectures in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**Davos**. Common shorthand for the World Economic Forum's Annual Meeting held in Davos, Switzerland each January. The WEF Young Global Leaders programme is documented in Part XIII Chapter 3 as the principal political-class convening architecture.

**Defence Innovation Board / NSCAI**. The US Defense Innovation Board (1994-2024) and the US National Security Commission on Artificial Intelligence (2018-2021) — institutional bodies through which the senior US technology executives have been integrated with US Department of Defense and intelligence-community policymaking. Referenced across the SCOTUS and IC dossiers and in the Part XIII Chapter 3 AI-policy capture analysis.

**Demons**. The volume's term for the contemporary senior figures operating the state apparatus. The political class. Volume Two dossiers 82 contemporary demons across the global record. The demons are not metaphor — the term names the named senior operators of the contemporary state-apparatus layer.

**Devil, the**. The volume's term for the four-century debt-money creation mechanism by private banks at compound interest. The source-and-root issue from which the state apparatus operates. Set out at full register in Volume One; restated in this volume's Part XIII Chapter 1 and across the closing chapter.

**DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency)**. The Musk-Ramaswamy-Vance-led advisory body established by Trump executive order 20 January 2025. Dossier 16 documents the technology-capital integration with the second Trump administration.

**Federal Reserve**. The US central bank. Founded 23 December 1913 following the Jekyll Island private meeting of November 1910. Dossiered in Volume One Chapter 4; referenced throughout Volume Two in the contemporary political-class chapters.

**Federalist Society**. Founded 1982. The principal US conservative-legal organisation. Documented judicial pipeline that has placed six of the nine sitting US Supreme Court justices and a clear majority of federal appellate judges in the judicial seats of the country. Dossier 15 documents the architecture jointly with the Heritage Foundation.

**Five Eyes**. The intelligence-sharing arrangement between the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. Referenced across the IC dossier and the post-Cold-War US-wars chapters.

**Foundation funding**. The contemporary philanthropic infrastructure through which large private fortunes shape policy at the universities, research institutions, NGOs, and political-influence architectures. Gates Foundation ($70+ billion AUM), Soros Open Society ($25 billion), Rockefeller, Ford, Carnegie, and MacArthur are dossiered as the principal contemporary US foundations.

**Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero (GFANZ)**. The multi-trillion-dollar private-finance climate framework launched at COP26 in November 2021. Mark Carney co-founder. Referenced in the Carney dossier (Dossier 74) and in the climate-finance chapter.

**Goldman Sachs**. The senior US investment bank. Dossiered in Volume One; the Goldman-Treasury revolving door and the international Goldman-political pipeline through Draghi-Carney-Barroso-Zoellick-Turnbull are the second named String in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**Heritage Foundation**. Founded 1973. The senior US conservative think tank. The Heritage Foundation's Project 2025 was a 922-page blueprint published nineteen months before the 2024 American election, with named contributors drawn from the staffs of three previous Republican administrations. Dossier 15 documents the architecture jointly with the Federalist Society.

**ICJ (International Court of Justice)**. The principal UN judicial body. The ICJ provisional-measures rulings on South Africa v Israel (January 2024, May 2024) are referenced in the Gaza chapter and in Dossier 81.

**ICC (International Criminal Court)**. The international criminal-justice court established by the 1998 Rome Statute. The November 2024 ICC arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant are referenced in the Gaza chapter and in Dossier 80.

**Intelligence Community (IC)**. The 18-agency US intelligence apparatus. Dossier 14 documents the senior IC principals and their institutional positions.

**Iran-Iraq War (1980-1988)**. The eight-year war between Iran and Iraq, with documented US-Western arms supply to both sides. 1-1.5 million dead. Part XII Chapter 3.

**Likud**. The senior Israeli right-wing political party. Discussed in the Israeli political-class dossiers and in the Gaza chapter.

**Mask, the**. The volume's term for the surfaces — the state apparatus, the political class, the captured press, the captured academic apparatus — that conceal the four-century debt-money mechanism from the populations it operates on. Volume Two documents the state mask. The mask is not metaphor — the term names the operational surfaces of the contemporary architecture.

**NATO (North Atlantic Treaty Organization)**. The US-led military alliance founded April 1949. Dossier 78 documents the post-2024 Trump-era realignment.

**Operation Condor**. The multinational state-terror network coordinated between the military regimes of Chile, Argentina, Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, and Uruguay, with American knowledge, during the 1970s-1980s. Referenced in the Latin American dossiers and in the historical political-class chapters.

**P5 (UN Security Council Permanent Five)**. The five permanent members of the UN Security Council with veto power: US, UK, France, Russia, China. Dossier 75 documents the contemporary veto architecture.

**Palantir**. Founded 2003 by Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Joe Lonsdale, and Stephen Cohen. The principal contemporary US data-analytics-and-surveillance company. The November 2024 Anthropic-Palantir-AWS partnership is documented in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**Political class**. The volume's principal subject: the operators who occupy the positions through which the state apparatus produces wars, surveillance, regulatory capture, and the upward redistribution of wealth the volume catalogues. The Demons.

**Project 2025**. The 922-page Heritage Foundation policy blueprint published April 2023. Discussed at length in Dossier 15.

**Proxy war**. A war between two or more states fought through one or more third-party state or non-state actors. Part XII Chapter 7 documents the contemporary proxy-war architecture.

**Reagan-Thatcher consolidation**. The 1979-1989 transformation of the Anglo-American political-economic order through deregulation, financialisation, and the dismantling of the post-war social settlement. Discussed in the historical political-class chapters and in the contemporary US-UK political-class dossiers.

**RSS (Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh)**. The Hindu-nationalist paramilitary-and-cultural organisation founded 1925. The foundation of the contemporary BJP government. Dossier 23 documents the RSS-VHP-Sangh Parivar architecture.

**SCOTUS (Supreme Court of the United States)**. The senior US judicial body. Dossier 13 documents the contemporary bench and the Federalist-Society pipeline that produced six of the nine sitting justices.

**Stargate Project**. The $500+ billion AI infrastructure programme announced at the White House on 21 January 2025 by Donald Trump, Sam Altman (OpenAI), Larry Ellison (Oracle), and Masayoshi Son (SoftBank). Discussed in Dossier 16 and across the Part XIII Chapter 3 AI-policy capture analysis.

**Strings, the**. In the Volume Two synthesis (Part XIII Chapter 3), the specific named institutional pathways through which the Devil (capital) operates through the Demons (the political class). Ten Strings are named: financial-political pipelines (BlackRock-Biden, Goldman-Treasury, international Goldman-political, UK financial-political); convening architectures (Davos, Bilderberg, CFR, Trilateral); funding architectures (Gates, Soros, Rockefeller, Ford, Carnegie, MacArthur, donor-advised funds); media architectures (Bezos, Sulzberger, Murdoch, Powell Jobs); and AI-policy capture (Stargate, the Anthropic and OpenAI White House meetings, Sacks, Krishnan).

**Trilateral Commission**. The senior North-America-Europe-Japan-Asia private convening organisation founded 1973 by David Rockefeller. Referenced as one of the contemporary convening architectures in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**Ukraine war (2022-)**. The Russian-Ukrainian war that began with the full-scale Russian invasion of February 2022. 400,000-550,000 dead as of the volume's writing. Part XII Chapter 4 documents the war architecture.

**UN OHCHR / OCHA**. The UN Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights and the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs. Referenced across the war chapters and in the Acknowledgements.

**WEF Young Global Leaders programme**. The World Economic Forum's senior-political-class development pipeline, founded 1993, that has produced contemporary heads of state and government across multiple jurisdictions including Trudeau, Macron, Ardern, Marin, Baerbock, and additional senior contemporary political-class figures. Dossiered in Part XIII Chapter 3.

**WHO (World Health Organization)**. The UN specialised agency for international public health. Dossier 82 documents the post-2025 US withdrawal under the second Trump administration.

**World Bank**. Founded 1944 at the Bretton Woods Conference. Dossier 76 documents its contemporary architecture jointly with the IMF and BIS.

**WTO (World Trade Organization)**. Founded 1995. Dossier 77 documents the post-2017 multilateral trade-architecture paralysis.

**You are the assessor of last resort**. The volume's standing framing — the reader holds the final adjudication authority on whether the record sustains the volume's positioning. Applied across the series and at full register in Volume Five Chapter 28.

---

# Index of Names

Each entry gives a brief identifying note and the dossiers in which the person is treated. Persons named only incidentally in passing are not indexed; the consolidated sources back matter lists the underlying documentary references.
